data.output:
Manage beads — portable, ephemeral work items with metadata.

Beads provide a lightweight work queue for AI agents and developers.
They track tasks, dependencies, and status without coupling to any
specific workflow methodology.

Examples:
  ddx bead init                                    # Initialize bead storage
  ddx bead create "Fix auth bug" --type bug        # Create a bead
  ddx bead list --status open                      # List open beads
  ddx bead ready                                   # Show beads ready for work
  ddx bead dep add <id> <dep-id>                   # Add a dependency
  ddx bead import --from jsonl beads.jsonl          # Import from JSONL

Usage:
  ddx bead [flags]
  ddx bead [command]

Aliases:
  bead, beads

Available Commands:
  ac-check              Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree
  archive               Archive closed beads when active beads.jsonl exceeds size threshold
  blocked               List externally blocked beads
  clear-cooldown        Bulk-clear work cooldowns across the tracker
  close                 Close a bead
  cooldown              Inspect and clear work cooldowns
  create                Create a new bead
  dep                   Manage bead dependencies
  doctor                Detect (and optionally repair) beads.jsonl rows with oversized fields or back-edge deps
  evidence              Manage append-only execution evidence
  export                Export beads as JSONL
  human                 Deprecated alias: resolve proposed operator-attention beads
  import                Import beads from external source
  init                  Initialize bead storage
  lint                  Check acceptance criteria mechanical verifiability
  list                  List beads
  merge                 Resolve a Git conflict in .ddx/beads.jsonl
  metrics               Per-bead cost, token, and attempt summaries from execution evidence
  migrate               Split active beads.jsonl into beads + beads-archive + attachments
  needs-human           Deprecated alias: list proposed operator-attention beads
  operator-attention    List wedge/timeout lease releases needing operator attention
  queue                 Manage in-priority queue ordering
  ready                 List beads ready for work
  reap                  List (or close) epics whose children are all terminal
  reconcile             Reconcile stale bead lifecycle metadata
  reconcile-attachments Commit dirty attachment audit files
  reopen                Reopen a closed bead
  replay                Re-execute a prior attempt with alternate model/harness/prompt
  replay-bench          Run multiple replays in parallel against the same source attempt
  review                Generate a review prompt for a bead's implementation
  routing               Show routing decisions recorded for a bead
  show                  Show a bead
  status                Show bead counts and active work
  update                Update a bead
  validate-ready        Validate execution-ready beads have machine-measurable acceptance

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for bead

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
      --no-update-check            Disable the automatic update check (network call) on each command
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx bead [command] --help" for more information about a command.

---UPDATE HELP---
Update a bead

Usage:
  ddx bead update <id> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    New acceptance criteria
      --assignee string      New assignee or claim assignee fallback
      --claim                Claim: set status=in_progress, assignee=ddx
      --description string   New description
  -h, --help                 help for update
      --labels string        New labels (comma-separated)
      --notes string         New notes
      --parent string        New parent bead ID
      --priority int         New priority
      --set stringArray      Set custom field (key=value, repeatable)
      --status string        New lifecycle status (validated transition)
      --title string         New title
      --unclaim              Unclaim: set status=open, clear assignee and claim fields
      --unset stringArray    Unset custom field (key repeatable)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
      --no-update-check            Disable the automatic update check (network call) on each command
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

14. lefthook run pre-commit passes (including the new no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths rule).","closing_commit_sha":"0826fac9fb709179b22a74b24cf9108623b7ef20","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:16:44.121242708Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-06cbaa90","depends_on_id":"ddx-77eb084e","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:33:22Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-06cbaa90","depends_on_id":"ddx-5cc92777","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T07:58:30Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

DEPS

  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.","events_attachment":"ddx-06eb05d8/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-06eb05d8","issue_type":"task","labels":["kind:refactor","phase:2","reconciled:no-changes-state","refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result","updated_at":"2026-06-08T04:25:18.437797228Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:4:{"closing_commit_sha":"2511a0b0de58e7ef136c5a7b032b2c0719103609","created_at":"2026-06-27T22:49:40.744689961Z","description":"PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","events_attachment":"ddx-071e9bd6/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-071e9bd6","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:server","area:agent","area:workers","kind:feature","kind:reliability","phase:build","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-24a3da84","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-49c6ecb8","spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"server: launch managed worker subprocesses with persisted PID/PGID","updated_at":"2026-06-30T07:39:05.267950702Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:8:{"closing_commit_sha":"81958ef5baab9362ad0e85b1c481c0c5a20a4c21","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02.143711599Z","description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` retried parent bead `ddx-c191e91a` after the parent had already been decomposed and marked execution-ineligible. On 2026-05-15 the first attempt `20260515T081727-1e2bf23c` preserved a no-change decomposition/parent update result, and `ddx bead show ddx-c191e91a --json` reported `execution-eligible: false` plus labels `area:agent,area:execution,kind:cleanup,decomposed`. `ddx bead ready --execution` no longer listed the bead. Despite that, the same live watcher started a second implementation attempt at `20260515T082640-623a9c00` against the same parent, burning model time and showing false progress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1073-1113` claims the candidate selected from the queue snapshot without a post-selection guard that rechecks execution eligibility after tracker changes made by a prior attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1681-1696` passes the originally selected `candidate` into `agenttry.Attempt`, so a stale candidate can still execute even if the store row has since been parked. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2367-2408` appends the terminal result and continues watch mode after a preserved/land-conflict/no-change result, allowing an immediate retry. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2603-2627` delegates selection to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, but the live incident proves this path can still select or retain an execution-ineligible decomposed parent. `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:131-134` documents the intended invariant: successful decomposition creates children and sets the parent `execution-eligible=false`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution-eligibility recheck immediately before claim and again before invoking `agenttry.Attempt` in the watch/drain loop. If a candidate is now execution-ineligible, decomposed, superseded, or otherwise absent from the fresh execution-ready view, release/skip it without dispatching an implementer and continue to the next ready bead or idle. Ensure preserved no-change decomposition and mixed no-change/land-conflict outcomes do not allow the same live watcher to retry the parent in the same loop run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go, only if needed for fixture reuse

NON-SCOPE
Do not change merge policy or preserved-iteration storage. Do not re-enable execution of decomposed parents. Do not hide land conflicts generally. Do not close the parent automatically unless existing child-closure policy already authorizes it.

DEPS
None. `ddx work --watch` can reject every pre-claim readiness result when `readiness_checks[].verdict` is emitted as a JSON bool instead of the consumer's expected string shape. In the observed Fizeau queue, each ready bead produced the same `pre_claim_intake.warn` decode error and the worker idled indefinitely despite ready work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The readiness classification decoder consumes `readiness_checks` through `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80`, then uses `failedReadinessReasons` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:823-834` to interpret failed checks. The contract must explicitly tolerate bool, string, null, and absent verdict values at this boundary; otherwise producer/consumer schema drift turns a readiness payload into `intake_error` before a bead can execute.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement or verify a custom `readinessVerdict` decoder that accepts `true`, `false`, strings, `null`, and absent verdicts, coercing to a documented canonical form (`true` -> `pass`, `false` -> `fail`, string -> trimmed string, null/absent -> empty). Ensure the classification decode path exercises that type and that failed-check detection still treats canonical `fail` as the failure signal.

NON-SCOPE

Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, CI schema locking, README documentation, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not rely on `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/...` as load-bearing test input; inline fixtures in tests. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed
    assertions) MUST be removed or rewritten as no-network tests.
  - Add a regression test TestLand_IsNetworkFree in
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go that wires a
    LandingGitOps with no Fetch/Push methods (proving they are no longer
    called) and asserts Land() succeeds.

  Operator-side note (NOT in scope of this bead): users push with raw
  `git push origin main` after a drain session. A future ddx sync helper
  is downstream of multi-node coordination work and is explicitly
  deferred.

NON-SCOPE
  - Designing/building a ddx sync helper (deferred until multi-node).
  - Federation work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is single-node
    only.
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact; file separate beads if any
    surface friction).
  - Changing TD-031 bead state machine.
  - Reducing the bead.Store WithLock primitive (different layer).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on doc-only spec amendment bead ddx-3d5197d8 landing first.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-c019e48a (now scoped to local-only stale-lock

AxonBackend is still a local JSONL emulation instead of a GraphQL-backed RawBackend. Before mutations can be implemented safely, the backend needs a real client boundary and bead/event row mapping that uses the generated Axon client types.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20 explicitly says the implementation serializes collections to JSONL files under <dir>/axon/.
- cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:71 defines AxonBackend around BeadsFile, EventsFile, and LockDir instead of a GraphQL client/session.
- cli/internal/bead/axon/generated.go exists, but axon_backend.go does not use the generated client path.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce the GraphQL client dependency boundary for AxonBackend using cli/internal/bead/axon generated types.
- Implement bead row and event entity encode/decode helpers against the generated GraphQL shapes, preserving the two-collection model ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events.
- Keep JSONL as the default backend and keep the existing experimental gate behavior unchanged.
- Add unit tests for client construction/mapping that do not require a live Axon server.

NON-SCOPE
- Full CRUD mutation implementation; child bead handles write operations.
- Backend selection config plumbing; existing follow-up handles selection.
- Broad conformance matrix; child bead handles it after operations exist.","events_attachment":"ddx-208a5bee/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-208a5bee","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:beads","area:storage","kind:feature","axon","from:ddx-9c5bca8f"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-9c5bca8f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec_id":"TD-030","status":"closed","title":"bead/axon: add GraphQL client boundary and row mapping","updated_at":"2026-05-06T02:54:30.960210861Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-06T02:53:26.740939803Z"}

Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_PreClaimDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, pre-claim fingerprint/threshold facts, and operator-attention reason for the repeated warning family.
- Existing related behavior from `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered by reuse/alignment with its helpers or by waiting on that dependency; this bead does not add a duplicate pre-claim warning escalation implementation.

NON-SCOPE

.ddx/beads.jsonl:33:{"acceptance":"1. New test TestServerStartupReconcile_ReportsStaleDesiredProjectAudit seeds a stale desired-state or ServerState project entry and proves startup reconcile exposes an audit/error event for the skipped or pruned project.
2. TestServerStartupReconcile_ReportsStaleDesiredProjectAudit proves the audit/error payload includes the exact stale project path.
3. TestServerStartupReconcile_ReportsStaleDesiredProjectAudit proves no audit/error is emitted for valid desired worker state for active projects.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"fc968fe9986576541faeaf7f416b9b2419606d27","created_at":"2026-06-16T19:42:19.447790662Z","description":"PROBLEM
When startup reconcile encounters stale desired-state or persisted ServerState project entries, operators need observable evidence of the skip or prune decision. Without an audit/error event carrying the project path, stale entries are hard to distinguish from worker launch failures or normal restart recovery.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/server.go:384-416 discovers desired worker project roots from server WorkingDir and persisted ServerState projects, but stale ServerState/desired-state entries can be ignored, retried, or failed without a dedicated observable audit/error event tied to the stale project path.
- cli/internal/server/server_startup_reconcile_test.go:96-204 does not assert that stale desired-state/project entries produce audit/error output with the affected project path.

PROPOSED FIX
Emit a startup-reconcile audit/error event whenever a desired project is skipped or pruned as stale. The event must include the stale project path and enough reason text/status for operators to diagnose missing, invalid, or non-live desired projects.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement GraphQL worker count/cap changes from ddx-c713d75e. Do not change bead claim semantics or Fizeau routing. Do not remove valid desired worker state for active projects such as /Users/erik/Projects/ddx and /Users/erik/Projects/sesame. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-operator-workbench. Parent bead: ddx-8674fc2d.","events_attachment":"ddx-20faafde/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-20faafde","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:iterate","area:server","area:workers","kind:bug","reliability","plan:server-operator-workbench"],"parent":"ddx-003b10ac","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-bb4db8ab","status":"closed","title":"server: audit stale desired project skips during startup reconcile","updated_at":"2026-06-16T20:39:46.008553833Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:34:{"acceptance":"1. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_SerializesGitOperations asserts both instruction variants contain a direct prohibition on parallel git/staging/commit operations and require sequential git add/git commit handling.
2. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_RequiresStagedPreCommitGate asserts both instruction variants say lefthook run pre-commit must be rerun after staging when staged files drive hooks and that no-staged-files runs are insufficient evidence.
3. Prompt snapshot or guardrail coverage is updated so future prompt edits preserve both rules.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestExecuteBeadInstructions_(SerializesGitOperations|RequiresStagedPreCommitGate)|TestExecuteBeadInstructionsText' -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4be990cdd93a48ddec8392515627ec360d85decd","created_at":"2026-05-15T22:29:19.941605037Z","description":"PROBLEM
During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, two Codex execute-bead workers recovered from avoidable tool-sequencing errors. For ddx-b6859802, the worker staged ordinary files and ignored evidence with parallel git add calls; one failed with: fatal: Unable to create ... index.lock: File exists. For ddx-bb8e7d07, the worker ran lefthook run pre-commit before staging and got a no-staged-files skip, then had to rerun after staging; it also attempted to interrupt a completed test-poll session. These recoveries waste worker time and can look like DDx dirty-tree failures even when the implementation is otherwise fine.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2337-2341 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2369-2373 tell rich-prompt and embedded-agent workers to run tests, stage specific paths, and commit once. They do not explicitly prohibit parallel git/index operations, do not say that lefthook run pre-commit must be run after staging when hooks are staged-file-sensitive, and do not warn that a no-staged-files hook run is insufficient acceptance evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText to require git/staging/commit commands to run sequentially, not through parallel tool calls.
- Require workers to rerun lefthook run pre-commit after staging the exact commit set when pre-commit depends on staged files; an earlier no-staged-files hook run is not sufficient AC evidence.
- Add or extend prompt guardrail/snapshot tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_prompt_snapshot_test.go so the rule cannot regress.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change core gitlock recovery.
- Do not change execute-bead lifecycle, result schema, or merge policy.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS

same project and worker B reaches `FinalizeDurableAudit` at the same moment
that worker A holds the tracker lock, worker B times out, and the loop
classifies the timeout as `operator_attention` and exits — even though
nothing requires operator action. The other worker just needs to finish.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain with two concurrent workers:
- Worker1 (pid 424293) was committing tracker state for niflheim-996afcc8.
- Worker2 (pid 681801), simultaneously finalizing niflheim-e43f04bb (an
  ADR-051 cold-tier PUT concurrency implementation that had already run to
  completion — 66091 tokens, 32m8.5s), hit `tracker lock timeout (max
  elapsed, lock: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock,
  owner pid: 424293)` while trying to commit durable audit outputs.
- Worker2 exited with `operator attention required`, releasing
  niflheim-e43f04bb back to `open` without committing the audit trail,
  and leaving these paths dirty: `.ddx/beads.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/attachments`.

The work was successful — 32 minutes of agent time produced a clean
implementation and commit. The lock race nuked the audit attribution and
forced the worker to stop. Re-running it would either repeat the agent run
(burning more tokens) or hit `already_satisfied` (with the worktree-leak
documented in `ddx-274943fc`).

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1309`
(`finalizeDurableAuditOrStop`) calls `runtime.FinalizeDurableAudit` and
branches on `isTransientGitContention(err.Error())` to decide retry vs
operator-attention. The matcher at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1019-1034` covers `.git/index.lock`
(via `gitlock.IsIndexLockError`) and the strings "unable to write new index
file", "cannot lock ref", "unable to update the ref" — but **does not cover
the DDx-specific tracker-lock error format** emitted by
`cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:243`:

  "tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)"

The tracker lock is a DDx-internal ordering lock around tracker writes
(`.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`), held briefly during tracker-state mutations.
Concurrent worker contention on this lock is structurally identical to
the `.git/index.lock` case the existing matcher handles, and the comment
on `isTransientGitContention` explicitly states the design intent:

  "A persistent cause (e.g. ENOSPC) simply keeps releasing+retrying the
   bead, which is still preferable to halting the whole unattended drain."

PROPOSED FIX: Extend `isTransientGitContention` to recognise the DDx
tracker-lock-timeout string class. Recommended implementation:

  1. Export a stable marker constant (or recogniser) from
     `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go` rather than string-matching
     across packages. Suggested: a sentinel error type
     `TrackerLockTimeoutError` wrapping the existing fields (lockDir,
     ownerPID), with an `Is(err)` helper.
  2. Update `FinalizeDurableAudit` and its callers to wrap the timeout
     error with this sentinel, so callers can use `errors.Is` instead of
     substring matching.
  3. In `isTransientGitContention` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1019`, add a
     branch that returns true for `errors.Is(err, TrackerLockTimeoutErr)`.
     Keep the existing substring fallbacks for defense in depth.
  4. Confirm via test that worker2 in the observed scenario survives the
     lock timeout, re-enters the loop, and successfully commits on a
     subsequent iteration when the lock releases.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing the tracker-lock retry policy itself (timeout duration, backoff
  curve) — only changing how the loop classifies the lock-timeout outcome.
- Changing concurrent-worker safety guarantees on tracker writes.
- Adding queue-level fairness or worker coordination (out of scope for
  this bead).
- Fixing the orphan-worktree leak that compounds this issue — tracked
  separately in `ddx-274943fc`.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go (export sentinel error type)
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go (wrap timeout with sentinel where
  it bubbles up — verify by reading line 41 and 127)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (extend `isTransientGitContention`)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go (new test for tracker-lock
  classification)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- gitlock package (already correct for .git/index.lock; do not modify)
- cli/cmd/work.go (exit-reason rendering unchanged)
- Any other isTransientGitContention call sites — only the durable-audit
  branch matters for this bug.","events_attachment":"ddx-2189a07f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2189a07f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:bead","area:execution","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"needs-human-reason":"consecutive_wedge","needs-human-since":"2026-05-31T06:52:49Z","needs-human-source":"ddx work","needs-human-suggested-action":"investigate the repeated wedge (resolver hang / phase-empty heartbeats) and clear the operator-attention state once resolved","needs-human-summary":"bead wedged on 2 consecutive claims (>= threshold 2; last wedge "progress_watchdog"); stopped re-claiming and flagged for operator attention","priority":0,"queue-rank":1250,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-eb508f6b","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: treat 'tracker lock timeout' as transient git contention (currently misclassifies concurrent-worker lock race as operator_attention and exits)","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:32.571303648Z"}

DDx has a live bead-store split-brain across bead commands, work planning, and targeted execution. On 2026-06-13 after installing 75d5c4e84, from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx-provider-failure-20260613: `ddx work` executed and closed JSONL-backed bead ddx-81e3adcd, and `rg ddx-81e3adcd .ddx/beads.jsonl` finds it at .ddx/beads.jsonl:316, but `ddx bead show ddx-81e3adcd --json` returns `bead: not found`. Conversely, `ddx bead show ddx-b53eb508 --json` sees a newly filed bead, but `ddx try ddx-b53eb508` returns `bead not found`. Operators cannot trust queue state, cannot target priority work, and cannot force provider/process failure bugs to the top if read/write/execution paths observe different stores.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- cli/cmd/bead.go:291-322 resolves bead commands through CommandFactory.beadWorkspaceRoot() and beadStore(). In this worktree it resolves to a store that sees ddx-b53eb508 but not JSONL bead ddx-81e3adcd.
- cli/cmd/work_plan.go:82-91 uses f.beadStore() for planning, but live `ddx work plan --json` still listed JSONL-backed ddx-81e3adcd while `ddx bead show` could not inspect it.
- cli/cmd/try.go:177 constructs `bead.NewStore(resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot))`; live `ddx try ddx-b53eb508` failed with `bead not found` despite `ddx bead show ddx-b53eb508 --json` succeeding from the same cwd. Additional try call sites at cli/cmd/try.go:263, :384, :444, and :515 also construct stores separately.
- cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go:41-48 is the shared root helper, but commands are still mixing beadWorkspaceRoot(), resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot), and direct ddxroot.JoinProject-style paths elsewhere.

PROPOSED FIX

Centralize bead store resolution for bead show/list/ready/export/update/create, work plan/work picker, work status/focus, and try behind one project-aware resolver that returns both the selected state path and a diagnostic explaining why. Add regression coverage for a repository with both an in-tree .ddx JSONL store and an alternate configured/global store so show, ready, plan, work, and try all see the same bead IDs and lifecycle state. Ensure `ddx try <id>` can execute every bead visible in `ddx bead show <id>`, or fails with a typed store-mismatch diagnostic that includes both physical store paths.

NON-SCOPE

Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl. Do not remove support for alternate bead backends. Do not rewrite execute-bead history. Do not treat this as only a display bug; command behavior must converge.","events_attachment":"ddx-231dff55/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-231dff55","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:beads","area:work","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","store-resolution","plan:server-managed-workers"],"notes":"2026-06-13 dogfood rerun of ddx-ef9df563 failed in ExecuteBeadWithConfig before dispatch: result.json reported 'loading bead ddx-ef9df563 from worktree snapshot: bead: not found'. Under multi-worker operation, a ddx work worker EXITS with "operator attention required: DDx could not commit durable audit outputs; resolve the git failure before continuing autonomous work" when the durable-audit/tracker commit hits transient .git/index.lock contention. Observed 2026-05-27: with 2 ddx workers + a fizeau worker + the server sharing one project .git, a worker died on `git add` tracker -> ".git/index.lock: File exists ... no owner found but lock is fresh (age 19s < 30s)". A transient lock conflict stops the whole worker; the more workers, the more often this fires, directly defeating unattended steady-state drain.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- git .git/index.lock carries no pid, so gitlock.IndexLockOwner finds no owner. RecoverGitIndexLock (cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:144-158) only reaps an ownerless lock once age >= StaleAge (30s, gitlock.go:26); below that it returns Removed=false ("no owner found but lock is fresh").
- RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery retries only RecoveryAttempts=3 (gitlock.go:30) with LiveOwnerWait=500ms (gitlock.go:34) ~= 1.5s total, far short of the 30s reap floor, then returns the index.lock error.
- commitTrackerLocked (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2153) calls runGitWithIndexLockRecovery for `git add`; its error becomes "staging tracker: ...". That propagates to the durable-audit-commit exit at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1175, which stops the worker (operator attention) instead of treating transient lock contention as retryable.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Do NOT exit the drain on transient .git/index.lock contention during the durable-audit/tracker commit. Classify index.lock contention as transient (mirroring the pre-claim tracker-contention class from ddx-df77e668), release the bead, and continue the loop so the next iteration retries. A transient lock must never stop a worker.
2. AND/OR raise the recovery patience for tracker/audit commits to a bounded backoff that can outlast typical contention (up to ~the StaleAge window) before failing.
3. Optional: reap a provably-orphaned lock sooner (e.g. confirm no live git holds the repo via lsof) while keeping the 30s floor when liveness is unknown.
Recommend (1) as the primary fix: transient lock contention is retryable, not operator-attention-worthy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken safety against removing a live git process index.lock.
- Do not change the landing-path read-tree retry (already has backoff, ddx-7e659c95).

CROSS-REF: same multi-worker-resilience theme as ddx-57c40485 (lock scoping) and ddx-df77e668 (pre-claim tracker-contention). This is the post-fix gap on the COMMIT side.","events_attachment":"ddx-23ac2796/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-23ac2796","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","prevention:ddx-57c40485","prevention:ddx-df77e668","priority:reliability","severity:high"],"notes":"Already fixed by operator direct-fix (bootstrap-hostile to dogfood): transient index.lock during durable-audit commit now classified retryable via gitlock.IsIndexLockError, worker continues instead of exiting. Landed 48631b2ed; TestWork_IndexLockContentionDuringAuditCommitIsTransientNotFatal + regression pass; full internal/agent gate green; reinstalled.","priority":0,"queue-rank":1010,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: worker exits on transient index.lock contention during durable-audit commit instead of retrying — kills unattended multi-worker drain","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:31.462993595Z"}

A server-managed worker must be fully cleanable. Stop, watchdog reap, restart, and server shutdown must not leave ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants behind.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 says WorkerManager manages in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, but no OS process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID and :1282-1286 terminates a process group only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 has the same limitation in watchdog reap. Because server-owned workers are often goroutines with PID zero, the server cannot guarantee child harness cleanup.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a server-managed process boundary. Preferred implementation: spawn a child ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group and record PID/PGID in WorkerRecord. If a transitional in-process path remains for tests, every harness child process group must be registered on the worker handle and killed through the same cleanup helper. Stop/reap/shutdown should send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, SIGKILL remaining descendants, release bead claims, persist terminal status, and be idempotent.

NON-SCOPE

.ddx/beads.jsonl:45:{"closing_commit_sha":"0d611fc6e087a5a8530c446f95051ad64c0bfa73","created_at":"2026-05-16T04:25:58.514552656Z","description":"PROBLEM
The drain-queue golden tests are failing because the workers page now exposes more than one heading named `Workers`, and the Playwright spec is using a non-unique role query. `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` calls `getByRole('heading', { name: 'Workers' })`, but the page also renders `Reported workers`, which makes the selector ambiguous in strict mode.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` uses a heading locator that collides with the page’s section heading.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:1-120` and its rendered output define the page structure that exposes both the main page heading and the workers subsection heading.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the drain-queue test target the unique page heading or otherwise disambiguate the query so the golden flow can verify the worker dispatch and terminal-state assertions. Keep the fix confined to the drain e2e spec and any minimal accessibility label adjustment required to make the heading unique.

NON-SCOPE
- Worker dispatch semantics.
- GraphQL or backend worker state transitions.
- Other e2e specs.

ACCEPTANCE
1. Both drain-queue golden tests in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` pass.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","events_attachment":"ddx-25d551f5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-25d551f5","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:frontend","kind:test","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":720,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-4d956f1e","status":"closed","title":"fix drain-queue golden selectors for the workers page","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:30.286094669Z","work-failed-routes":[{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-16T07:14:02Z","count":2,"model":"Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit","provider":"grendel-omlx","reason":"provider_connectivity"},{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-16T05:46:59Z","count":1,"model":"mlx-community/Qwen3.6-27B-8bit","provider":"grendel-rapid-mlx","reason":"provider_connectivity"}]}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:48:{"created_at":"2026-06-29T10:45:26.873700708Z","description":"PROBLEM
Observed on 2026-06-29 in this repo while draining the queue. Worker pid 4014720 hit 4 consecutive 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes within ~2h on different beads (ddx-9aa2151d, ddx-95d80f7e, ddx-1c865bdb, ddx-aef568e7) without any successful merge. Load average was 38-52 throughout (8-core host). After each transport failure the worker advances to the next ready bead and the cycle repeats; closed counter unchanged across the period.

Specific log signature:
  do response complete in <N>m
  tracker_lock section=durable_audit wait=1ms hold=<seconds>
  ✗ ddx-<id> → transport: exit status 1

Some attempts had very short responses (1m37s) suggesting codex died early; others completed long (32m+) then died at durable_audit. Common factor: the provider subprocess exits status 1, which DDx classifies as 'transport' (not tests_red, not no_changes, not timeout).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The outcome classifier maps any non-zero provider exit (codex/claude/etc.) to 'transport: exit status 1' regardless of cause (OOM, kill, network error mid-stream). Inspect cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go around the outcome-mapping path for the codex/provider exit code (search 'transport: exit status'). The classifier does not distinguish 'load-killed' from 'genuine bug' from 'provider API error'.
- Under high system load (load avg > ~3-5x CPU count), the provider subprocess gets starved during the durable_audit / land phase (which is git+disk heavy), bumps into kernel OOM-killer or hits its own internal heartbeat/timeout, and exits non-zero. DDx then walks the queue trying the next bead which also dies, burning context+tokens with zero forward progress.
- There is no backoff/cooldown when transport failures cluster (e.g., 3+ in N minutes).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add load-pressure detection: before each claim, sample /proc/loadavg and compare to runtime.NumCPU(); if 5-min loadavg > k * cores (k=2-3 reasonable), emit a structured backoff observation and sleep proportional to overload before claiming.
2. Add transport-failure cooldown: when N (≥3) 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes occur within the last M minutes, the worker should pause for a longer interval (e.g., 5-15 min) before next claim instead of immediately consuming another bead.
3. Capture and surface the provider subprocess's exit reason where possible: check WaitStatus for signal-killed (SIGKILL → OOM-likely, SIGTERM → graceful), and distinguish in the outcome string (e.g., 'transport: provider killed by SIGKILL (likely OOM)').

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (outcome classifier + claim-loop pacing)
- cli/cmd/work.go (worker pacing/backoff)
- cli/internal/workerstatus/ (if load sampling needs a place)

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not change the queue ordering.
- Do not change the durable_audit commit flow itself.
- Do not implement a general-purpose host load manager.

AC
1. New helper LoadPressureSnapshot() in cli/internal/workerstatus or cli/internal/agent returning normalized loadavg/cores; unit test covers >threshold and <threshold paths.
2. Worker pre-claim path checks the snapshot; when pressure exceeds k (default 2.5), logs a structured 'load_pressure_backoff' observation and sleeps before claim. Unit test asserts the path is invoked with k configurable.
3. Worker tracks last N (≥3) 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes by wall-clock timestamp; when 3 occur within 10m, the next claim is delayed by an extended cooldown (>=5m). Unit test simulates 3 failures within window and asserts the delay.
4. Outcome classifier distinguishes signal-killed (SIGKILL/SIGTERM) provider exits in the outcome string. Unit test asserts the distinct outcomes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/workerstatus/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","route_reason":"explicit_pin_discovery_unavailable","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-06-29T12:01:21.452071062Z","kind":"routing","source":"legacy agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini reason=explicit_pin_discovery_unavailable"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"actual_harness":"codex","actual_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","actual_power":0,"actual_provider":"","attempt_id":"20260629T111036-3759f345","bead_id":"ddx-29491a0e","estimated_difficulty":"","rejected_route_pins":null,"requested_power_class":"standard","requested_profile":"default","routing_intent_degraded":false,"routing_intent_note":"","routing_intent_source":"default"}","created_at":"2026-06-29T12:01:23.447847957Z","kind":"execution-routing-intent","source":"ddx work","summary":"source=default powerClass=standard model=gpt-5.4-mini harness=codex"},{"actor":"erik","body":"{"base_rev":"6375c78abf32d01b479579a7b322559f68d4ff30","classification":"transport","detail":"exit status 1","rationale":"The attempt ended with harness/provider subprocess failure `exit status 1`, `result_rev` equals `base_rev`, and there is no evidence of code changes, test failures, merge conflict, quota, or bead-owned blocker. DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.880567652Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-358f2457","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-848069a3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-0b8780db","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-387a0178","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-7e1fed08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T16:13:04Z"}],"description":"Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.","events":[{"actor":"ddx bead doctor","body":"dependencies→removed dangling ddx-b669bb9f (1 edge(s))

  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.

7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"be4fdf4dc1228e1bc25506952c35f41c9fb4dadc","created_at":"2026-05-07T18:24:57.360955073Z","description":"PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.","events_attachment":"ddx-377ebf92/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-377ebf92","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:docs","area:specs","kind:design","operator-ux","phase:2","queue-steward","reconciled:no-changes-state","spec:FEAT-010","spec:FEAT-011"],"parent":"ddx-09d7d787","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010,FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"agent-modes spec: document interactive stewardship modes","updated_at":"2026-05-08T03:58:27.826005071Z"}

A 2026-06-19 doc/impl alignment audit surfaced several internal inconsistencies in docs/helix that are pure documentation defects (no code change). They are bundled here because each is a small, independently verifiable doc edit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
1. Triplicate TDs: docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-035-artifact-search.md, TD-038-artifact-search.md, and TD-039-artifact-search.md are three near-identical "Artifact Search Semantics" designs (same title, same depends_on [TD-026, TD-029]), all produced by the closed bead ddx-23cbcb4b from non-idempotent execution attempts each grabbing a fresh TD number. None is cross-referenced elsewhere.
2. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:11 has a self-contradictory status: '**Status:** Proposed (Accepted after operator review)'.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:18 cites the upstream contract as CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md, while audit docs (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md, AR-2026-05-02-fictional-upstream-deps.md) name it CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md.
4. FEAT status vocabulary across the 26 docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-*.md is internally inconsistent: 'Frame' vs 'Framing' (two spellings of one state) and 'Complete' vs 'Implemented' (used interchangeably).
5. A TD-030 design doc cites '/tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md' as load-bearing background, violating CLAUDE.md (no /tmp/ plan citations; they do not survive between machines).

PROPOSED FIX
Make each edit; no code changes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the canonical FEAT Status enum to match HELIX's [Draft|Specified|Approved] (DDx intentionally tracks build progress); only make the existing vocabulary self-consistent. .ddx/beads.jsonl:63:{"acceptance":"1. Add TestWorkerSet_RetriesDuringRestartListenerReadiness covering cli/cmd/worker.go: it simulates a local server URL that refuses the first connection or two during restart, then accepts PUT /api/agent/workers/desired, and asserts `ddx worker set` succeeds without operator retry.
2. Add TestWorkerSet_ReadinessRetryBudgetExpires or equivalent coverage asserting persistent connection refused returns a clear error naming the local server readiness timeout.
3. If the fix moves server address publication instead of client retry, add or update a server test proving `server.addr` is not advertised as ready until the listener is accepting connections.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"8161d43ed0f94dcceedc087720e765dabc3d48e3","created_at":"2026-06-16T17:04:32.944744079Z","description":"PROBLEM
Immediately after `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` on 2026-06-16, `ddx worker set --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx --count 1 --restart` failed with `server request: Put "https://127.0.0.1:7743/api/agent/workers/desired": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:7743: connect: connection refused`. Retrying after the listener accepted requests succeeded. Operators should be able to issue worker control commands immediately after a managed service restart without a manual sleep/retry loop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/server.go:351-363 writes `server.addr`, calls `reconcileDesiredWorkersAfterStartup`, and only then enters `http.ListenAndServeTLS`. During that window clients can resolve the restarted server URL but the listener is not accepting connections yet.
- cli/cmd/worker.go:103-110 sends a single PUT to `/api/agent/workers/desired` and returns the raw connection error without bounded readiness retry when the recorded server is alive but not yet listening.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:373-382 intentionally delays startup reconcile passes, so service startup is already asynchronous; the worker-control CLI needs a matching readiness contract or the server needs to write/advertise readiness only after bind.

PROPOSED FIX
Make server restart readiness deterministic for worker-control commands. Either bind/listen before writing the advertised ready address, or add a bounded retry/readiness wait in worker control for connection-refused failures against the local server immediately after restart. Keep failures fast for non-local, TLS/auth, or persistent server-down cases, and surface an actionable error if the readiness budget expires.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change desired worker reconciliation semantics, worker count caps, or GraphQL worker mutations. Do not mask real HTTP 4xx/5xx responses. Do not alter startup desired-worker restore behavior except as required to make listener readiness observable.","id":"ddx-386328e3","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:server","area:workers","kind:bug","reliability","plan:server-operator-workbench"],"parent":"ddx-8674fc2d","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"server: tolerate listener readiness after restart","updated_at":"2026-06-16T17:14:45.681720268Z"}

  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:65:{"acceptance":"1. `README.md` includes a section for pre-claim intake and silent-idle diagnosis that documents the canonical verdict forms, warn vs operator-attention vs hard-error mapping, and operator commands/logs to inspect when a ready queue idles.
2. `TestReadmeDocumentsPreClaimIntakeContract` asserts the README mentions `readiness_checks`, bool/string/null-or-absent verdict handling, `loop.operator_attention`, same-fingerprint or repeated idle escalation, and claim-success-rate warning knobs.
3. The docs preserve the parent cross-references to AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context, ddx-57c40485, and ddx-8f2e0ebf as related reliability context without making them part of this implementation scope.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestReadmeDocumentsPreClaimIntakeContract` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"27cd54b45cc90f099179fc62f48e00d4bd119613","created_at":"2026-05-27T05:50:46.191479049Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators lack a concise, durable guide for diagnosing a `ddx work --watch` loop that appears alive but does not claim ready beads. The parent incident's suggested action pointed to route/harness configuration even though the actionable fault was DDx's readiness decoder/worker behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The user-facing README is the natural top-level diagnostic surface, but the pre-claim intake contract and silent-idle escalation behavior need to be stated there near the quick-start operational commands in `README.md:40-45`. Worker behavior is implemented in the readiness classification and work loop, but operators should not need to read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` to know what warn-only, operator-attention, and hard errors mean.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the README to name the `readiness_checks` contract, canonical verdict forms, failure-class mapping, same-fingerprint/operator-attention behavior, claim-success-rate warning knobs, and first diagnostic commands/logs for a full ready queue that is idling. Keep the guidance implementation-neutral enough to remain valid as the specific worker internals evolve.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change runtime behavior, decoder behavior, schema validation, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not add long incident narrative or depend on the external Fizeau log path. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
Depends on the decoder, same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warning, and schema-lock children so the docs describe landed behavior.","events_attachment":"ddx-387dd863/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-387dd863","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high"],"parent":"ddx-f9ddaa68","priority":0,"queue-rank":1000,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f10a29fb","status":"closed","title":"Document pre-claim intake contract and silent-idle diagnosis","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:31.420546498Z"}

TD-027 requires the foundational Store mutation surface to accept context explicitly, but Create, Update, and Close still accept untyped variadic arguments. This hides cancellation behavior and forces the original ddx-464feba2 bead to mix Store API changes with a repo-wide caller migration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:86-89 declares BeadLifecycle with Create(args ...any), while Apply already uses ctx-first shape.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:517-596 exposes Store.Create(args ...any), scans for *Bead, and calls GenID through the variadic path.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:627-655 exposes Store.Update(args ...any), scans for id and mutate function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1499-1535 exposes Store.Close(args ...any), scans for id and then calls lifecycle helpers without explicit caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the mutation-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead), Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)), and Close(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Create, Update, and Close callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior and typed argument validation.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Create, Update, and Close.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and read signature children.","events_attachment":"ddx-38801c65/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-38801c65","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:bead","area:storage","kind:refactor","phase:iterate","spec:TD-027"],"parent":"ddx-3fd19af1","priority":0,"queue-rank":1750,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-3f203198","status":"closed","title":"bead store: migrate mutation APIs to ctx-first signatures","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:34.694186066Z"}
