The unattended ddx work drain halts with "worker exited: operator attention required" when a tracked skill SYMLINK is materialized into a real directory by an external skill-install/sync, leaving the project worktree dirty with a non-tracker path. Observed 2026-05-27 06:26: a continuous ddx worker closed 3 beads, then on next pre-claim found .crush/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter dirty — git tracks it as a symlink (mode 120000) but the working tree had it as a directory (SKILL.md/assets/scripts) — and exited via the uncommitted-implementation-changes guard. Operator had to restore the symlink (rm + git checkout) and restart the worker. A stray tool-managed skill dir stops the whole queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2480 emits "operator attention: project worktree %s has uncommitted implementation changes; released %s" and exits the drain when the landing worktree has any non-allowlisted dirty path (message at execute_bead_loop.go:955-957).
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:38-48 trackerMetadataPaths only allowlists DDx-managed files (added by ddx-df77e668). Tool-managed skill symlinks under .crush/skills/ (and likely .claude/skills, .agents/skills) are not covered, so their symlink->dir materialization reads as implementation dirt and halts the drain.

PROPOSED FIX (implementer/operator picks)
A. Pre-claim: treat a tracked symlink materialized into a dir whose content matches the symlink target as benign (optionally auto-restore the symlink). RECOMMENDED.
B. Pre-claim: allowlist tool-managed skill-dir roots (.crush/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills), analogous to trackerMetadataPaths.
C. Repo hygiene: gitignore + untrack these paths (currently committed as symlinks; deliberate structural change).

NON-SCOPE: do not weaken the guard for genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edits; do not change the external skill tooling.
CROSS-REF: extends the tracker-file allowlist from ddx-df77e668.","events_attachment":"ddx-3885cce5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3885cce5","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:pre-claim","kind:bug","kind:reliability","priority:reliability","observability","prevention:ddx-df77e668"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-a337178f","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: unattended drain halts on tool-managed skill-symlink churn (.crush/skills/*); make pre-claim resilient","updated_at":"2026-05-28T09:04:00.873222728Z","work-failed-routes":[{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-28T03:23:56Z","count":1,"endpoint":"http://bragi:1234/v1/chat/completions","model":"qwen3.5-27b","provider":"bragi","reason":"provider_connectivity","timeout_class":"bad gateway"}]}

2. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:17:59.120820292Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"readiness route .ddx/beads.jsonl:69:{"acceptance":"1. The prose checker no longer hard-fails on a non-exact-match Vale version: it either accepts a compatible range or auto-installs the pinned Vale into a ddx cache dir.
2. A test in cli/internal/docprose (e.g. TestValeVersionAcceptsCompatibleRange or TestValeBootstrapResolvesBinary) covers the new behavior without requiring a preinstalled exact vale.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/docprose/... green
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-38c58f5b","created_at":"2026-05-28T15:00:46.884617521Z","description":"PROBLEM: The ddx prose checker requires an EXACT Vale version string and neither accepts a compatible range nor installs Vale itself. Operators without precisely that build are hard-blocked, with no bootstrap.
ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/docprose/vale_runner.go:13 SupportedValeVersion="vale version 3.13.0"; cli/cmd/doctor.go:24 const supportedValeVersion + :631 errors when version != supportedValeVersion (exact-string equality); vale_runner.go:62 exec.LookPath("vale") only — no install/bootstrap. No version-range tolerance and no self-install.
PROPOSED FIX: either (a) accept a documented compatible version range (e.g. >= 3.13.0 within the 3.x line) instead of exact-string equality, or (b) bootstrap the pinned Vale (download the pinned release into a ddx-managed cache dir and invoke that) so the check is self-sufficient on a clean machine. Prefer (b) with (a) as fallback.
NON-SCOPE: changing Vale styles/rules.","events_attachment":"ddx-38c58f5b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-38c58f5b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:cli","area:checks","kind:bug","operator-ux","reliability"],"priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"doctor/prose: Vale check requires exact pinned version and self-installs nothing — make it flexible or bootstrap Vale","updated_at":"2026-05-28T16:53:19.423005243Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:70:{"acceptance":"1. `TestCleanupCommand_JSONIncludesProcessFindings` proves `ddx cleanup --json` includes process findings/counts and still exposes the stale temp dir, run-state, and scratch fields that already existed.
2. `TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsProcessFindingsInText` (or equivalent) proves dry-run text includes process findings/counts and still prints the existing stale temp dir/run-state/scratch summary lines.
3. The existing cleanup command regression tests, especially `TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting` and `TestCleanupCommand_JSONShape`, continue to pass with the widened output contract.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'CleanupCommand|Cleanup.*Process|AttemptDescendant' -count=1` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes and `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"110a5eafa6c6be40528ad7e9d7a176c15ff87cf9","created_at":"2026-06-28T00:49:17.44884232Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-392ed1e4","depends_on_id":"ddx-ae98c450","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-28T00:49:17Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
`ddx cleanup` can already report stale temp dirs, run-state files, scratch dirs, and evidence retention, but it has no way to show stale attempt-descendant process findings to the operator. A dry-run can therefore look clean while the host still has DDx-owned CPU/port pressure.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/cleanup.go:11-30` defines `cleanupCommandReport` with only file/resource counters, warnings, blocked errors, and observations; there is no process field in the JSON/text contract.
`cli/cmd/cleanup.go:69-72` constructs `agent.NewExecutionCleanupManager` and runs it, but `runCleanup` only copies the current file/resource summary fields into the report and prints those fields in text output.
`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` currently exposes file/resource counts only, so the CLI has nothing process-related to forward.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend `cleanupCommandReport` with process counts and structured process findings from the cleanup summary, keeping the existing resource counters intact.
- Update `runCleanup` so both `--json` and the text report include process findings/counts without dropping stale temp dir, run-state, scratch, or evidence fields.
- Keep dry-run semantics unchanged: the report should show what `--apply` would terminate, but dry-run must not kill anything.
- Add focused command tests that assert the widened JSON/text shape while preserving the current resource-reporting assertions.

NON-SCOPE
- Process detection/reaping logic itself lives in the sibling `agent` bead.
- Startup/pre-claim cleanup wiring lives in the sibling `work startup` bead.
- Documentation updates in `docs/` are handled separately.
- No change to the command name, flag set, or non-cleanup subcommands.","events_attachment":"ddx-392ed1e4/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-392ed1e4","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:cleanup","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","operator-ux","resource-exhaustion","incident:niflheim-2026-06-08","spec:FEAT-010","spec:SD-025"],"parent":"ddx-2f8e5f26","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-fcff5472","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"cmd: surface cleanup process findings in JSON and text","updated_at":"2026-06-30T10:55:58.614062039Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:71:{"acceptance":"1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
2. The regression shows the worker preserves or reconciles land state instead of rerunning implementation when the land result is already effectively landed or blocked by dirty staged evidence.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'LandCoordination' -count=1` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f11e2988d00ff1d9376a839b6bf450969e4c303a","created_at":"2026-06-08T06:13:37.906342882Z","description":"PROBLEM
Landing failures that are really ref-lock, already-landed, or staged-evidence coordination issues still look like ordinary implementation failures to the worker. That causes fresh implementation reruns and the wrong retry lane instead of reconciliation or operator attention.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:292-312` wraps every `LandingAdvancer` error as a generic `land advancer` failure, which loses whether the candidate was already landed, whether `refs/heads/main` could not be locked, or whether the landing worktree was dirty. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report_test.go:57-62` only proves the current detail string records land failure; it does not distinguish the coordination class from implementation failure.

PROPOSED FIX
- Split land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures into explicit retry actions: reconcile, retry-land, or operator-attention.
- If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and continue without rerunning implementation.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:73:{"closing_commit_sha":"bf8f35171539bed89108e3264d4cd0836c449850","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:00.307694448Z","description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

DDx/FIZ worker failures can still surface as underspecified execution failures when provider availability, model availability, credentials, endpoint reachability, or harness configuration differs across machines. DDx is the abstraction that should let unattended workers continue through provider variance; a generic provider failure that gives no typed cause, no fallback action, and no durable route-health evidence is a product bug.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:287-290 returns pre-dispatch Fizeau Execute errors as a generic `agent: execute: %w`, leaving the execute loop without a typed provider/model/configuration failure contract.
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:332-376 copies final service error text into Result.Error/ExitCode, but downstream classification still depends on free-text heuristics rather than a required typed route-health outcome for provider unavailable, model unavailable, auth missing, quota/rate limit, endpoint timeout, or harness misconfiguration.
cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:516-544 and :588-612 contain the unpinned route-exclusion/escalation path, but that path only works when the failure is already recognized as provider_connectivity/no_viable_provider; untyped provider/configuration failures can stop or preserve the bead without proving fallback was attempted or intentionally impossible.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define a typed provider failure taxonomy at the DDx/Fizeau boundary: provider_connectivity, provider_auth, provider_rate_limit, provider_quota, provider_model_unavailable, provider_harness_unavailable, provider_config_invalid, no_viable_provider, and unknown_provider_failure.
- Normalize both pre-dispatch Execute errors and final failed events into that taxonomy before ExecuteBeadReport construction.
- For unpinned workers, convert retryable provider/model/harness availability failures into route-health evidence and continue fallback/escalation without parking the bead.
- For explicit operator pins, fail loudly with a typed hard-pin exhausted/operator-action report that names the pin and available remediation, without silently widening pins.
- Persist durable evidence on the bead/run showing requested constraints, resolved route if any, typed failure, retryability, whether fallback was attempted, and why fallback stopped.

NON-SCOPE
Do not move concrete route selection into DDx; Fizeau still owns routing, aliases, model discovery, and provider catalog behavior. Do not remove explicit operator pins. Do not call real providers in tests.","events_attachment":"ddx-3b721804/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3b721804","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","area:work","area:routing","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":"-120","schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-9a2b4976","spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"agent: type provider failures and enforce fallback semantics","updated_at":"2026-06-13T18:38:23.850216294Z"}

Spec saves from the GraphQL API can still overwrite a document when the caller's optimistic concurrency token is stale, and the current mutation schema exposes only path and content. The resolver does rebuild the graph after writes, but there is no explicit stale-write gate or conflict response before the filesystem is mutated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34-55 defines DocumentWrite with only path and content and writes directly with os.WriteFile.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3313-3319 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without an expected hash/version input.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:62-89 rebuilds the graph after the write, but only after the filesystem has already been changed.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an expected hash/version argument to documentWrite in schema.graphql and thread it into resolver_mutation_docs.go.
- Compare the caller's expected token against the current document before os.WriteFile; if it mismatches, return a conflict instead of overwriting.
- Keep ResolveDocumentPath as the confinement gate for absolute paths, traversal, and symlink escapes.
- Preserve the post-save graph rebuild and return the refreshed document/graph state after a successful write.
- Add focused resolver tests for stale-save refusal, confinement refusal, and graph refresh.

- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent work` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "work is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent work`, and deprecation text still names work rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `work` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `work-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent work` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent work`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `work-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent work` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.

During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, a worker executing ddx-bdc0065e cancelled several stale beads. Multiple ddx bead update commands ran close together from the execute-bead context. The tracker ended clean, but the auto-commit audit trail was noisy: one commit subject said it updated ddx-387a0178 while it also picked up additional bead-row changes, another update failed with .git/index.lock, and another commit reported nothing to commit. That makes tracker history misleading and is the same class of dirty/index-lock noise the operator wants eliminated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:134-154 builds bead auto-commit config and calls bead.AutoCommitFilesWithRecovery directly. cli/internal/bead/git_autocommit.go:10-17 wraps git add/commit with gitlock.RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery, but that only recovers from stale/transient .git/index.lock; it does not serialize the whole add/commit pair under the process-shared main git lock. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:98-118 documents and implements withMainGitLock/withTrackerLock for primary-checkout git writes, and cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:22-24 says DDx writers serialize through that lock, but the bead CLI auto-commit path is outside it.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route bead CLI auto-commit add/commit operations through the same process-shared main git lock used by landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
- Preserve existing stale index.lock recovery inside the lock, so stale locks are still repaired and live owners still fail or wait according to policy.
- Add a concurrent regression where multiple bead update/create auto-commits run against one repo and produce either deterministic serialized commits or one explicitly combined commit; no index.lock failures, no misleading commit subjects that omit paths actually included.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change .ddx/beads.jsonl format.
- Do not remove auto-commit recovery for stale index locks.
- Do not squash, amend, or rewrite existing execute-bead history.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885):

1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go):
     Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149
     GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167
     ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182
     ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224
     Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518
     Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598
     Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627
     Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426
   And in archive.go:
     GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172
   Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts
   either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today.

2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first
   parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in
   cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying
   AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`,
   etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in
   external packages.

3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external
   callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead";
   "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent,
   cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.")
   and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead."

4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs
   `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129).
   That build covers every package in the module, including all the
   external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching.

5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today
   (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`):
     - 174 Go files reference these Store methods.
     - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg
       `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37,
       cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/...,
       cli/internal/server/...).
     - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too:
         cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0])
         cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...)
   `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one
   of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/
   GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is
   no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current
   call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a
   single receiver type).

6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that
   preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170
   files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server,
   internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the
   "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That
   is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split
   (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason.

      pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd,
      cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server,
      cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics.

  (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test
      ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and
      explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit
      will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The
      follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is
      complete.

  (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or
      retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first
      Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the
      legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration",
      then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the
      caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027
      (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package
      first) without breaking the build outside the package.

No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged
strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work
outside the worktree.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:90:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/ -timeout=180s -count=1 -run='^(TestListProviders|TestShowProvider|TestProviderUnknownStateContract|TestMCPProvider)' ` completes under the timeout (previously hung indefinitely on TestListProviders alone).
2. `internal/agent/serviceconfig.go` exports `NewServiceFromWorkDirCtx(ctx context.Context, workDir string) (agentlib.FizeauService, error)` that sets `agentlib.ServiceOptions{QuotaRefreshContext: ctx}` so fizeau-spawned goroutines terminate when ctx is cancelled.
3. All callsites in `internal/server/` and `internal/server/graphql/` that previously called `agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir` / `agent.NewStatusProbeServiceFromWorkDir` / `agentlib.New(agentlib.ServiceOptions{})` for ad-hoc service construction are migrated to ctx-aware variants. Verified by `grep -rn 'NewServiceFromWorkDir\b\|agentlib.New(agentlib.ServiceOptions{})' cli/internal/server/` returning only the test-stub callsite and doc-comments.
4. `cli/internal/server/providers.go` adds a `providerHandlerTimeout = 10 * time.Second` and wraps `svc.RouteStatus` / `svc.ListHarnesses` calls in a detached-goroutine + ctx.Done() race so a slow / hung pi subprocess does not block the handler beyond the bound.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -timeout=180s -count=1` green on an unloaded system (suite completed in 224s under loadavg 37 locally, vs hang at 600s previously).
6. `cd cli && go vet ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` clean.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-30T06:57:51.474535193Z","description":"PROBLEM: `go test ./internal/server/...` hits the 600s test timeout. The goroutine dump shows hundreds of leaked fizeau aliveness / quota-refresh / refresh-scheduler goroutines waiting in `select { <-ctx.Done() }`, and the test driver goroutine itself is stuck in `svc.RouteStatus(ctx)` → `subprocessHarnessAutoRoutingModels` → `pi --list-models` subprocess wait. Under CI / loaded systems the synchronous subprocess wait inside `RouteStatus` exceeds any reasonable per-test budget, compounding with the goroutine accumulation.

ROOT CAUSE:
1. `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:88` (`NewServiceFromWorkDir`) and `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:96` (`NewStatusProbeServiceFromWorkDir`) construct the fizeau service with `agentlib.ServiceOptions{}` — leaving `QuotaRefreshContext` as `context.Background()`. Inside fizeau (`service.go:1079`), this drives `startAlivenessProbeLoop`, `startQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop`, `startPrimaryQuotaRefreshWorker`, and `refreshScheduler.Start(startupCtx)`, all of which spawn goroutines that only terminate on ctx cancel. Every short-lived HTTP / MCP / GraphQL request that constructs a fresh service leaks ~6 goroutines for the lifetime of the test binary.
2. `cli/internal/server/providers.go:108`, `providers.go:113`, `providers.go:130`, `providers.go:142`, `providers.go:391`, `providers.go:411` — `listHarnessInfos` and `liveRouteStatusReport` use the bare-`Background` factory. `svc.RouteStatus(ctx)` internally invokes `pi.DefaultModelSnapshot` at `fizeau@v0.14.33/internal/harnesses/pi/runner.go:352`, which hard-codes `context.Background()` when shelling out to `pi --list-models` — meaning the caller's ctx deadline does not propagate.
3. Same pattern leaks at `cli/internal/server/agent_models.go:38`, `agent_models.go:138`, `cli/internal/server/workers.go:853`, `workers.go:868`, `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:83`, `resolver_providers.go:99`, `resolver_providers.go:193`, `resolver_providers.go:309`, and `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_provider_models.go:176`.

PROPOSED FIX:
- Add `agent.NewServiceFromWorkDirCtx(ctx, workDir)` and `agent.NewStatusProbeServiceFromWorkDirCtx(ctx, workDir)` in `internal/agent/serviceconfig.go` that pipe ctx into `ServiceOptions.QuotaRefreshContext`. Document the goroutine-lifecycle caveat on both the legacy and ctx-aware factories.
- Update all 11 server-package callsites (HTTP handlers, MCP wrappers, GraphQL resolvers, worker manager) to use the ctx-aware factory. HTTP / MCP handlers get a bounded 10s timeout (`providerHandlerTimeout`) so a slow upstream subprocess cannot stall the request indefinitely.
- Wrap `svc.RouteStatus` and `svc.ListHarnesses` calls in `internal/server/providers.go` in detached goroutines bounded by ctx, since the upstream library still ignores ctx in some subprocess paths (pi/runner.go:352).
- Fix the one in-test direct `agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(dir)` call in `internal/server/providers_test.go` to use the ctx-aware variant with a `t.Cleanup`-bound cancel.

NON-SCOPE:

A parent-death signal is not sufficient as the only guard: existing orphaned execute-bead harness trees from prior dead workers can remain under PID 1, and platforms without Linux Pdeathsig need a startup backstop. `ddx work` needs to clean up orphaned harness children from prior dead workers without killing harnesses owned by live workers or unrelated projects.

- The parent bead identifies the needed integration with lease-release/operator-attention behavior as cross-ref ddx-8f2e0ebf, but no startup reaper currently adopts that responsibility.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a `ddx work` startup reaper that identifies execute-bead harness processes from prior dead workers, scoped to the current project/workspace using durable ownership evidence rather than broad command-name matching alone.

NON-SCOPE

ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the identical warn-only rejection ("decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string"), so the worker logs a warning and skips. With a full ready queue and a single fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue goes idle indefinitely. Fizeau observation 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC: identical fingerprint 15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f recurred across distinct bead_ids, confirming a schema mismatch rather than a bead-specific issue.

ROOT CAUSE
The verdict field in the readiness-check struct is typed as a plain Go string, so encoding/json rejects a bool payload. See cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:77-82 (preClaimReadinessCheck struct) and the decode entry point decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult / decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResultWithMode at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:709-725. Producers emit verdict as a JSON bool (true/false) while the consumer parsed string only.

PROPOSED FIX (parent proposed-fix A)
Introduce a custom readinessVerdict type at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:95-125 with an UnmarshalJSON that accepts JSON bool, JSON string, and null/absent, coercing each to a single documented canonical form (e.g. bool true -> "pass", false -> "fail", string lowercased, null/absent -> empty). Wire the preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict field to the new type so the array/object payload decoder at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416 succeeds for every verdict shape.

NON-SCOPE
- CI schema drift gate (sibling child: schema lock).
- Same-fingerprint warn escalation / warn-only 100%-failure guard (sibling child: escalation).
- claim-success-rate operator signal (sibling child: claim-rate window).
- README documentation (sibling child: docs).

CROSS-REF
Governing artifact: AR-2026-05-17 follow-up beads (blocked by this idle). Adjacent silent-failure bugs in this session: ddx-57c40485 (index.lock held minutes), ddx-8f2e0ebf (worker wedges on route resolution). Shared theme: silent failures inside the worker that look healthy from the outside.","events_attachment":"ddx-436dd452/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-436dd452","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","kind:bug","kind:reliability"],"parent":"ddx-f9ddaa68","priority":0,"queue-rank":1100,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-69d66ffe","status":"closed","title":"pre-claim intake decoder: accept readiness_checks[].verdict as JSON bool, string, or absent","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:31.831282391Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

When a worker abandons a bead (route-resolution timeout, watchdog fire, or any failure release), the bead's claim/lease must be cleared atomically and `ddx bead status` must reflect the lease state. Claiming a bead today atomically transitions it to in_progress, but there is no symmetric release path that, in a single locked write, transitions the bead back and clears the lease. The parent bug (ddx-8f2e0ebf) observed "bead has a claim but ddx bead status shows In progress: 0" — claim and lifecycle status not atomically coupled on the release side.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go: Store.Claim (~lines 863-930) is atomic — under WithLock it calls transitionLifecycleInPlace(StatusInProgress) + sets Owner + WriteAll, then writeClaimHeartbeat writes the lease sidecar. There is NO symmetric Store.Release that, under the same WithLock, transitions status back (default open), clears Owner, WriteAll, AND removes the claim-heartbeat lease sidecar in one critical section. Failure-release paths can leave a stale lease sidecar while status reports not-in_progress, producing the phantom-lease inconsistency.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add Store.Release(id, assignee, targetStatus) mirroring Store.Claim: under WithLock, read beads, transitionLifecycleInPlace to target status (open by default), clear Owner, WriteAll, and remove the claim-heartbeat lease sidecar — all in one locked operation.
2. Route the worker's failure-release paths through Store.Release so claim clearing and status are coupled.
3. Ensure `ddx bead status` counts reflect lease state: a released bead is not counted in_progress and has no owner; a claimed bead is in_progress with an owner.

NON-SCOPE
Route-resolution timeout (sibling child A), heartbeat watchdog (child B), same-bead-twice guard (child D), operator-attention query surface + docs (child E). This bead only establishes atomic claim/release symmetry and status accuracy. This is a decomposition child of ddx-8f2e0ebf.","events_attachment":"ddx-449baa1d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-449baa1d","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:routing","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability"],"parent":"ddx-8f2e0ebf","priority":0,"queue-rank":910,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-ca25ffb9","status":"closed","title":"bead: add atomic Store.Release so failure-release clears the claim and status reflects the lease","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:31.047985079Z"}

When an operator wants to retry a bead currently in cooldown (work-retry-after set in the future), the only way today is to manually run ddx bead update <id> --unset work-retry-after before each ddx try / ddx work invocation. With 14 beads in cooldown today the operator ran 14 such commands in sequence (and even then a single ddx bead clear-cooldown --all doesn't exist yet — that's ddx-63688d7b). There is no CLI surface for "I know this bead is cooled; run it anyway because I just resolved the underlying issue."

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/work.go and cli/cmd/try.go both honor work-retry-after via the standard ready-queue filter. There is no override flag. The escalation ladder and route-exclusion machinery is correct (and ddx-63688d7b will narrow when cooldowns even apply), but operators still need a per-invocation escape hatch for the cases where cooldown was set by a now-resolved condition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add --force-claim to ddx try <id>: ignore work-retry-after for the specified bead during this attempt. Emit a kind=force-claim event with actor=<assignee> and the timestamp the override was used. The cooldown field is NOT cleared; if the attempt fails again, the original cooldown remains in effect for queued workers.
- Add --ignore-cooldown to ddx work: drain mode, ignore work-retry-after across the queue for this worker session. Same event semantics.
- Both flags emit a structured event so the audit trail shows "operator forced through cooldown at time T".
- Both flags require an explicit --reason "<text>" string (operator must explain why). The reason is captured in the event body.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the cooldown-setting policy (ddx-63688d7b owns that).
- Do not auto-clear cooldowns after a successful forced attempt (the bead's success implicitly invalidates any stale cooldown via its own state, but we don't mutate the field).
- Do not expose this flag in passthrough constraints; it's an operator override only.

DEPS

`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:381-395` builds an `AgentRunRuntime` with `WorkDir: projectRoot` and dispatches the readiness model call through the normal service path.

Run all hidden lifecycle model calls (pre-claim intake, pre-claim decomposition, and post-ladder decomposition) in a DDx-owned scratch execution directory or otherwise enforce a read-only/no-write dispatch boundary. Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE

Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees asserts that with N pre-existing /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-* worktrees whose owning processes are dead and whose mtime is older than DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE, `ddx work --once` (cold start) removes them via git worktree remove + rm -rf before claiming any bead.
- TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees asserts that worktrees whose owning ddx-try/ddx-work process is still alive are NOT reaped.

`ddx work --watch` is currently a foreground process bound to the operator's terminal. When the terminal detaches (SIGHUP), the operator Ctrl-C's, or a transient git-index race trips the durable-audit commit, the worker dies and NOTHING restarts it. Queue drain stalls until the operator manually restarts. Observed live 2026-07-02: two consecutive worker deaths in one session, 17 ready beads stalled ~2h waiting.

The P0 epic `ddx-9d1af129` (server: supervise project workers from durable desired state) documents this. Phase 1 of `docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md` requires: desired-state model + persistence + reconcile loop.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:38-533` — the entire Phase 1 code exists: `WorkerDesiredState` model (line 38), `Validate` (line 109), `WorkerSupervisor` struct (line 147), `NewWorkerSupervisor` (line 157), `SaveDesiredState`/`LoadDesiredState` (line 174/204), `Reconcile`/`ReconcileAt` (line 229/234), `restartBackoffDelay` (line 437), `pruneTimeWindow` (line 468). All unit-tested at `cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go` (316 lines).

But `Reconcile()` is NEVER CALLED in production. `rg 'Reconcile\(\)' cli/ | grep -v _test` returns zero hits. `cli/internal/server/server.go:175` constructs a `WorkerManager` but never wraps it in a `WorkerSupervisor`. No background reconcile goroutine exists. No CLI writes `desired.json`.

# PROPOSED FIX
Wire the existing supervisor into server lifecycle:

1. **Bootstrap**: in `cli/internal/server/server.go` `New()` (after `workers := NewWorkerManager(workingDir)` at line 175), construct `supervisor := NewWorkerSupervisor(workers)` and hold it on the Server struct.

2. **Reconcile goroutine**: in `ListenAndServe` (line 311) or a new `Start` helper, spawn a background goroutine that calls `supervisor.Reconcile()` on a `time.Ticker` (10s default, configurable via env `DDX_SUPERVISOR_TICK`). Guard with a context cancelled by `Shutdown`.

3. **Shutdown integration**: in `Shutdown()` (line 285), cancel the reconcile context and wait for the goroutine before returning.

4. **CLI surface**: new `cli/cmd/worker.go` with subcommands:
   - `ddx worker set --project <path> --count N [--harness X] [--model Y] [--restart-enabled=true]` — writes `.ddx/workers/desired.json` via `supervisor.SaveDesiredState`. Works standalone (no server needed) since save is filesystem-only.
   - `ddx worker status [--project <path>] [--json]` — reads `desired.json` + queries running workers.
   - `ddx worker enable [--project <path>]` — shortcut for `--count 1 --restart-enabled=true`.

5. **Tests** (see AC).

# NON-SCOPE
- Subprocess boundary + process-group cleanup (`ddx-1c865bdb`, `ddx-24a3da84`, `ddx-096129b9`) — Phase 2, separate beads.
- GraphQL/UI wiring (`ddx-cf0cafef`, `ddx-2b15342f`) — Phase 3.
- Docker leak-proof (`ddx-6ae5134b`) — Phase 4.
- Dogfood migration (`ddx-f6609c33`) — Phase 5.
- ADR-022 amendment — track separately.

# IMPACT
After this bead lands and the operator sets `desired_count=1` via `ddx worker enable` and restarts `ddx server`, worker death (crash, SIGKILL, transient git race, terminal detach) is followed by automatic restart within one supervisor tick, honoring `WorkerRestartPolicy` backoff. The user no longer has to manually restart.","id":"ddx-4ab00c05","issue_type":"feature","labels":["area:server","area:workers","kind:feature","kind:reliability","phase:build","plan:server-managed-workers","supervisor:phase-1"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":3,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"server: wire WorkerSupervisor reconcile loop into server lifecycle (Phase 1 of ddx-9d1af129)","updated_at":"2026-07-02T06:05:32.351252042Z"}

When pre_claim_intake produces a warn-only rejection with the same fingerprint repeatedly (for example a readiness schema mismatch), the worker logs a warning and goes idle every cycle (pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle). With many ready beads and one daemon, none get claimed and the worker looks alive while making zero progress, leaving the queue idle indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE
- warn-only policy mode masks the failure: the loop emits pre_claim_intake.warn at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1859, :1982, :2009, :2034, :2073, each carrying a fingerprint computed in cli/internal/agent/readiness_decision.go:23-24, and then goes loop.idle (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1637-1649) with no guard counting consecutive identical fingerprints across distinct bead IDs to break the silent loop.
- The threshold knob exists at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:76-79 (ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero means DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) and the escalation message format at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1141.

PROPOSED FIX
After N (default DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes sharing the same fingerprint across distinct bead IDs, emit a loop.operator_attention event and a durable operator_attention bead event (reason "preclaim_warn_repeated", carrying fingerprint, count, distinct_bead_ids, example_detail, example_payload), stop before claiming the threshold bead, and leave the remaining ready queue untouched. The threshold is configurable via ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero falls back to the default.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the CI schema drift gate (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change verdict decoding or readiness payload schema.
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.

3. A Test* in cli/cmd/doctor_test.go asserts doctor renders both the "Global Install (...)" and "Project Install (...)" lines with the correct ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global statuses (doctor_test.go:273,276).

7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"bd11f3c9adebedc32b3639a97362f4129071bab6","created_at":"2026-06-03T01:02:41.360272672Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

First-time machine setup needs an umbrella `ddx init --global` that installs the default ddx plugin globally and wires agent-tier links, while plain `ddx init` must create only the project workspace and lazy-resolve plugins (preferring project over global) rather than eagerly installing. `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see where the skill resolves from. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09, already landed) must keep scoping skill checks to staged skill files regardless of install location.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:75 dispatches to initGlobal (init.go:318) when --global is set; initGlobal installs the bundled default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:320), creates symlinks ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx into that dir (init.go:336), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (init.go:351, writeGlobalConfig init.go:362).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create <project>/.ddx/ and NOT write plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via the registry resolver, preferring the project copy over global.
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:421-448 computes globalPath/projectPath and the projectStatus "lazy-resolves-to-global" (doctor.go:436-444) and prints the "Global Install (...) — <status>" and "Project Install (...) — <status>" lines (doctor.go:447-448).
- Before this, `ddx init` bundled plugin install with manifest creation, there was no global setup command, and doctor reported only a single install layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx links into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx, and writes the global config with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins; first use lazy-resolves from the project layer, falling back to global.
3. `ddx doctor` renders two lines: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing" and "Project Install (<projectRoot>/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global".
4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook continues to run skill-related validation only when skill files are staged, regardless of whether the install is global or project-scoped.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness skill paths beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Plugin content writing for non-init installs is owned by the install child beads.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global setup routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir(), project init through ddxroot.Path(). Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2). Related: ddx-777f4a09 (ddx-validate hook, landed).","events_attachment":"ddx-4c58ea3e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4c58ea3e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","area:plugin","initiative:zero-footprint","kind:feature","phase:3","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"queue-rank":1400,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-04beccf3","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: --global umbrella setup, lazy project init, and two-layer doctor reporting","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:33.205624891Z"}

Server shutdown must clean every server-owned managed worker process tree, not only background hubs and land coordinators. A graceful server shutdown can currently leave ddx work or harness descendants running.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/server.go:269-283 documents Shutdown as closing background services, then calls s.beadHub.Close and s.workers.LandCoordinators.StopAll. It does not stop WorkerManager work workers. cli/internal/server/workers.go:218-220 stores live worker handles, but there is no manager shutdown pass that iterates those handles and applies the Stop/reap process-tree cleanup path.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an idempotent WorkerManager shutdown method that snapshots server-owned managed work handles and on-disk running server-owned records, invokes the shared managed process-tree cleanup path, releases any claimed bead for those workers, and persists terminal stopped status. Call it from Server.Shutdown before or alongside LandCoordinators.StopAll so server shutdown cleans all managed worker process groups.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT AND RELATIONSHIP
Parent bead: ddx-9d1af129. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-managed-workers. No explicit dependency IDs were provided by the parent bead.","events_attachment":"ddx-4cb7fd2b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4cb7fd2b","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:server","area:agent","area:workers","kind:feature","kind:reliability","phase:build","plan:server-managed-workers","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-24a3da84","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-aa959742","status":"closed","title":"server: shut down all server-owned managed worker process trees","updated_at":"2026-06-30T10:04:58.772296285Z"}

- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.

DDx governing documentation still describes decomposed parent beads as open, execution-ineligible containers that depend on generated children. That contradicts the desired lifecycle contract: a losslessly decomposed bead is completed by decomposition, while children remain separately executable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:20-22 says `too_large_decomposed` leaves the parent `status=open` with dependency edges to children.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:63-73 says the parent remains open and depends on all children.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:162,169,171,180,197-207 says review/no_changes/decompose outcomes leave oversized or decomposed beads open with child dep edges or `execution-eligible=false`.
- The same contract is referenced by docs/beads.md, docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md, and FEAT-010 decomposition text where present.

PROPOSED FIX
Update docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, and matching FEAT-010 decomposition text so they state one normative rule: a lossless child/sibling/replacement decomposition closes the decomposed bead as completed-by-decomposition. Preserve traceability guidance through child Parent metadata and decomposition events, but state that Parent is not a dependency gate and generated children must not depend on the decomposed parent. Explicitly describe historical open `execution-eligible=false` containers as legacy/backfill cases only.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.
- Do not redesign epic auto-close or historical `dead_intermediate_close`; keep those as legacy/backfill behavior for already-open containers.
- Do not drain or close existing decomposed queue entries as part of this bead. File or run a separate reconciliation bead if historical tracker cleanup is needed.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Related, not dependency: ddx-130cf9ab.
Governing artifacts: docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, FEAT-010.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Deps: No deps. DDx should run prose checks during normal docs-changing attempts without users reminding the agent. Findings need to be captured as evidence and fed back while the attempt is still editable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts that change Markdown under `docs/` to run `ddx doc prose --changed`, attach evidence, and keep prose advisory. No workflow hook currently implements that contract.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Workflow-hook policy is high-judgment because it touches execution finalization and evidence. A weak implementation could block correct work on advisory prose findings or run too late for agents to repair obvious prose issues.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect docs Markdown changes in `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts.
- Run `ddx doc prose --changed` before finalization when docs changed.
- Attach prose-check evidence to the attempt.
- Feed findings back to the agent before final response when editable.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default and separate from correctness review.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make prose checks mandatory blockers.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.
- Do not alter Fizeau routing.

DEPS

store.go:Reopen (lines 1664-1665) and store.go:Unclaim (lines 1065-1067) maintain parallel loops that delete the same set of claim metadata Extra keys. This duplication is error-prone: if ClaimMetadataExtraKeys is extended, both loops must be updated.

ROOT CAUSE
store.go:Reopen clears claim fields in isolation; store.go:Unclaim does the same separately. Both iterate ClaimMetadataExtraKeys but the operations are not factored into a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Extract a private helper func clearClaimExtraKeys(extra map[string]any) that deletes all entries in ClaimMetadataExtraKeys from the Extra map.
2. Update store.go:Reopen:1664-1665 to call clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra).
3. Update store.go:Unclaim:1064-1069 to call clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra).
4. Verify both methods also call RemoveClaimHeartbeat as before (Unclaim does; Reopen should also).

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the set of keys being cleared. Do not refactor heartbeat cleanup.

The registry/defaultplugin layer still exposes .ddx/plugins/ddx as if it were the forward install target for the built-in DDx package. That makes compatibility metadata look like the normal marketplace/cache-backed topology and leaves tests asserting the wrong package shape.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:18-27 says generated adapters and payload copies are not forward repository state.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go:75-82 and cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/package.yaml:14-22 still expose install.root.target=.ddx/plugins/ddx for the built-in ddx package, while registry comments treat it as compatibility-only metadata.
- cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:60-68 asserts the legacy .ddx/plugins/ddx target.

PROPOSED FIX
Adjust registry/defaultplugin package metadata and tests so .ddx/plugins/ddx is either removed from forward assertions or explicitly classified as local-overlay/legacy compatibility metadata. Add tests that prove the built-in DDx package resolves through plugin lock/cache/defaultplugin fallback and that normal registry behavior does not require a project payload copy.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove support for explicit local development overlays. Do not remove the embedded default plugin fallback needed for offline ddx init. Do not change HELIX marketplace packaging.

Operators need an explicit startup signal so stale TLS or tsnet state does not keep confusing client/server trust debugging.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` previously allowed `~/.ddx/` to act as a project state root. High-risk server consumers include TLS cert/key state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:326` and tsnet state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:503`. Once the resolver falls through to XDG, those old home directories are no longer read by the server path helpers.

PROPOSED FIX: Add startup detection in the server path that checks for legacy `JoinHome("server")` and `JoinHome("tsnet")` directories when the active project state root resolves elsewhere. Emit a clear warning naming the orphan source and the active XDG/project state destination. Keep the behavior warning-only unless the implementation already has an established safe migration helper.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rename `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/`. Do not address multi-project federation collisions. Do not change TLS-renew scheduling or rotation logic. Do not silently delete legacy home-state directories. .ddx/beads.jsonl:132:{"acceptance":"See bead body — 5 verifiable AC items: new ddxroot regression test, ~20 test fixtures updated to write config.yaml, go test -short ./internal/... green, orphan-warning at startup, pre-commit clean.","created_at":"2026-06-01T21:09:49.582933254Z","description":"**The bug**

`cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` resolves a project's state root in
one of two locations based on whether `<projectRoot>/.ddx/` exists as a
directory:

```go
func Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {
    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)
    if info, err := os.Stat(inTree); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
        return inTree    // <projectRoot>/.ddx/
    }
    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))
    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)
    return root          // ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<identity>
}
```

The `.ddx/` directory test is too weak. It accepts ANY directory named
`.ddx/` as evidence that `projectRoot` is a DDx project. The home
directory `$HOME` happens to have a `~/.ddx/` directory because that's
where user-global state (`installed.yaml`, `plugins/`) lives via the
parallel `JoinHome()` helper. So if any `ddx ...` invocation is made
with `projectRoot=$HOME` (e.g., via `cwd=$HOME` and no `--project`
override), `Path()` returns `~/.ddx/` as if the user's home were a
project root.

Result: the same logical state files appear at two different paths
depending on how the binary was invoked, and the duplicates drift.
The concrete instance that triggered this investigation was the TLS
cert: `ddx server` running with `WorkingDir=/home/erik/Projects/ddx`
(via systemd) wrote its cert at
`/home/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem`, while a separate
invocation (probably manual, from `$HOME`) wrote
`/home/erik/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` — different fingerprint, same
filename, same purpose. The server served the project-root cert; some
other client trusted the home-dir cert; every connection got
`tls: bad certificate`.

**Audit — every consumer of the dual-path resolver**

`ddxroot.JoinProject` / `ddxroot.Path` is called from ~50 callsites
across the codebase. Every one of them inherits the bifurcation. The
highest-risk consumers (those that read or write state that must NOT
diverge between locations):

- `beads.jsonl` (`execute_bead.go:2165`, `execute_bead.go:2722`,
  `execute_bead_dangling_success.go:189`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:244`,
  `service_run.go:396`, `server.go:797`, `server.go:5043`, `exec/store.go:56`)
- `metrics/locks.jsonl` (`lockmetrics/lockmetrics.go:96`)
- `metrics/attempts.jsonl` (`attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:99`)
- `.git-tracker.lock` (`lockmetrics/lockcap.go:221`)
- `server/tls/cert.pem` + `key.pem` (`server/server.go:326`)
- `tsnet/` (`server/server.go:503`)
- `workers/<id>/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1370`, `workerstatus/liveness.go:44`,
  `executions_mirror.go:211`)
- `executions/<attempt-id>/` (`prose_evidence.go:125`,
  `reviewer_stream_artifact.go:41,45`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:23,27`)
- `agent-logs/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1268`)
- `config.yaml` (`init.go:155`, `execution_cleanup.go:221`,
  `execution_worktree_root.go:23`)
- `exec/definitions/`, `exec/runs/` (`exec/path_helpers.go:14,18`)
- `graphs/` (`docgraph.go:1197`)
- `run-state.json` + `RunStateDir` (`agent/run_state.go:60,64`)
- `tracker_lock.go:116` (the bead tracker lock)

Anything in this list reachable from two different `projectRoot`
arguments (or with one of them being `$HOME`) ends up duplicated.

**Proposed fix (structural)**

Tighten the in-tree check. A bare `.ddx/` directory is necessary but
not sufficient evidence of a project root. Require an initialization
marker: `<projectRoot>/.ddx/config.yaml` (the file written by
`ddx init` at `cli/cmd/init.go:236`). Patch:

```go
func Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {
    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)
    if isInitializedInTree(inTree) {   // was: os.Stat / IsDir
        return inTree
    }
    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))
    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)
    return root
}

func isInitializedInTree(dir string) bool {
    info, err := os.Stat(dir)
    if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {
        return false
    }
    _, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "config.yaml"))
    return err == nil
}
```

`ExistingPath` gets the same treatment.

This makes `Path($HOME)` correctly fall through to
`~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<identity>` (since `~/.ddx/` has no
`config.yaml`), eliminating the dual-location bifurcation entirely.

**Blast radius (this is what blocks landing the patch as-is)**

A surveyed initial spike against this patch made `internal/ddxroot`
tests pass, but exposed ~20 test fixtures elsewhere that build a bare
`.ddx/` directory and rely on the old "any `.ddx/` = project root"
contract. Failing packages from a quick `go test -short ./internal/...`
run:

- `internal/server/graphql` (9 failures: `TestAgentMetrics_*`,
  `TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject`, `TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject`)

Files that need their test fixtures updated to also write a `config.yaml`
in the `.ddx/`:

- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent_metrics_test.go` (fixtureWorkingDir)
- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_workingdir_ctx_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go` (3 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_interrupted_attempt_test.go` (2 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_commit_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go`
- `internal/agent/serviceconfig_test.go`
- `internal/agent/drain_regression_test.go`
- `internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go` (6 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go` (3 callsites)

A central helper (e.g., `testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, dir)`)
that creates `.ddx/` + writes a stub `config.yaml` would cap the diff.

**Migration**

Existing user-global state at `~/.ddx/server/tls/`, `~/.ddx/tsnet/` etc.
gets orphaned by the fix (they were created when `Path($HOME)`
returned `~/.ddx/`; afterwards Path uses the XDG path). The transition
is graceful for TLS (server regenerates a fresh self-signed cert) but
operators with manual config in those subdirs will need a one-shot
migration. Recommend:

1. Emit a startup warning when `~/.ddx/server/` exists alongside the
   XDG path, telling the operator to delete the orphan.
2. Or auto-move `~/.ddx/server/` → `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<host-id>/server/`
   on first server start under the new resolver.

**Operational note**

The original sindri-dev TLS bad-cert storm (~36 hours, blocking the
entire ddx work queue) was unblocked by manually copying the home-dir
cert+key over the project-root location and restarting ddx-server.
That's a band-aid; this bead is the proper unwind.

**Severity: P1**

The bug is latent across most state files. The reason it manifested as
"server unreachable" rather than "bead tracker corrupted" is that
nobody was running ddx commands as `$HOME` against beads files
recently. Any operator who does (or any future feature that uses
`projectRoot=$HOME` for any reason) will silently bifurcate state.

**Acceptance**

1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test
   (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/`
   without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.
2. All 20+ test fixtures listed above updated to write
   `.ddx/config.yaml` (or use the new testutils helper).
3. `go test -short ./internal/...` passes.
4. Startup or migration logic warns about orphan `~/.ddx/server/` /
   `~/.ddx/tsnet/` directories.
5. Pre-commit / lefthook green.

**Out of scope**

- Renaming `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/` (separate cleanup).
- Multi-project federation collision (different concern, different
  layer).
- The TLS-renew schedule / rotation logic (orthogonal — the bug is
  not "the cert was rotated"; it's "two carts exist").","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/` without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.","coverage":"Child 1 AC 1 covers `TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`; Child 1 AC 4 covers `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/ddxroot/...`."},{"parent_ac":"2. File: cli/cmd/install.go:244
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Registry installs explicitly create `.ddx/plugins/<name>` as a project-local symlink when the manifest root target is global (`~/...`) so project-relative plugin paths still resolve. The new manifest-driven local install path does not mirror that behavior: it creates the global symlink only. Repro: a local plugin whose manifest sets `install.root.target: ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin` produces `global_plugin_exists=yes`, `project_plugin_exists=no`, and still installs skills. Any code or docs that resolve plugin resources through `.ddx/plugins/<name>/...` now break in local developer installs.
Suggested fix: When `installLocal` honors a global root target, also create the same project-local `.ddx/plugins/<name>` symlink that `InstallPackage` creates for registry installs.","id":"hx-3f3c0e74","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep project-local plugin path for local installs with global roots","updated_at":"2026-04-10T04:33:09.938605658Z"}

File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.
Suggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.","id":"hx-4a12a619","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: keep routing metrics model-specific","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:47:24.607997607Z"}

Description: AutoCommit now changes the git working directory to filepath.Dir(filePath) but still runs git add with the original filePath.
