The runs/[runId] page (built in S16.1) has no Evidence tab. Users cannot browse or download execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json, stderr logs, etc.) from the run-detail view. Whitelisting logic for safe inline rendering must be enforced to avoid serving binary or executable content inline.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte (added by ddx-f04faa86) has no Evidence tab.
- No bundleFiles[] or bundleFile(path) resolver exists yet for the run-detail page (that resolver is ddx-6d132f62, a dep).
- No whitelist enforcement is implemented; any file could be rendered inline without size or extension checks.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add Evidence tab to runs/[runId]/+page.svelte (as a sibling tab to Overview/Prompt/Response/Tools/Session).
- List every file in run's bundle with: path, size, mimeType.
- Whitelist: *.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB — render inline as raw text.
- Non-whitelisted or > 64KB: download link only. No Markdown rendering, no syntax highlighting, no hex preview.
- Evidence tab lazy-loads on activation.
- Large files (> 64KB): show size + download button only.
- Bundle-root confinement enforced server-side on bundleFile(path) resolver.

NON-SCOPE
- Markdown rendering (deliberate: whitelist is raw-text only).
- Syntax highlighting.
- Hex preview of binary files.","events":[{"actor":"ddx bead doctor","body":"dependencies→removed dangling ddx-f04faa86 (1 edge(s))","created_at":"2026-06-08T04:25:18.443992133Z","kind":"repair","source":"ddx bead doctor --fix","summary":"doctor repair applied: 1 change(s)"}],"id":"ddx-4cd64068","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","phase:2","story:16"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-a8a18cb7","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-99bda416","status":"closed","title":"Story 16.2: Evidence tab — bundle-file list + download + whitelist inline view","updated_at":"2026-06-08T04:25:18.443992133Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T22:57:45.031771905Z"}

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-f9ddaa68 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the same warn-only rejection, so the worker logs a warning and skips. With 23 ready beads in the fizeau queue and one fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue is idle indefinitely.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC; about an hour after the previous wedge bug ddx-8f2e0ebf)
- Worker: PID 1527216, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 01:22:40 at observation.
- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779077845655338391.jsonl
- Pattern every ~50s: resource.preflight (status ok) -> pre_claim_intake.start -> pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle.
- Sample pre_claim_intake.warn payload:
  {
    "bead_id":"fizeau-5b2a8950",
    "decision":"warn",
    "decision_source":"pre_claim_intake",
    "detail":"decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string",
    "fingerprint":"15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f",
    "outcome":"intake_error",
    "policy_mode":"warn-only",
    "reason":"system_unready",
    "rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready",
    "suggested_action":"check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry",
    "system_reason":"unavailable"
  }
- Identical fingerprint across multiple bead_ids confirms it is a schema mismatch, not a bead-specific issue.
- Queue state at observation: open=44, ready=23, dependency-waiting=19. None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.
- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.
2. warn-only policy_mode masks the failure: the worker keeps polling and looks alive (loop.idle every 5

7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"8389873bf4f30aa82192e4960d76785bab8c5b33","created_at":"2026-06-17T20:04:26.18940515Z","description":"PROBLEM
DDx governing documentation still describes decomposed parent beads as open, execution-ineligible containers that depend on generated children. That contradicts the desired lifecycle contract: a losslessly decomposed bead is completed by decomposition, while children remain separately executable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:20-22 says `too_large_decomposed` leaves the parent `status=open` with dependency edges to children.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:63-73 says the parent remains open and depends on all children.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:162,169,171,180,197-207 says review/no_changes/decompose outcomes leave oversized or decomposed beads open with child dep edges or `execution-eligible=false`.
- The same contract is referenced by docs/beads.md, docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md, and FEAT-010 decomposition text where present.

PROPOSED FIX
Update docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, and matching FEAT-010 decomposition text so they state one normative rule: a lossless child/sibling/replacement decomposition closes the decomposed bead as completed-by-decomposition. Preserve traceability guidance through child Parent metadata and decomposition events, but state that Parent is not a dependency gate and generated children must not depend on the decomposed parent. Explicitly describe historical open `execution-eligible=false` containers as legacy/backfill cases only.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.
- Do not redesign epic auto-close or historical `dead_intermediate_close`; keep those as legacy/backfill behavior for already-open containers.
- Do not drain or close existing decomposed queue entries as part of this bead. File or run a separate reconciliation bead if historical tracker cleanup is needed.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Related, not dependency: ddx-130cf9ab.
Governing artifacts: docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, FEAT-010.

DDx should run prose checks during normal docs-changing attempts without users reminding the agent. Findings need to be captured as evidence and fed back while the attempt is still editable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts that change Markdown under `docs/` to run `ddx doc prose --changed`, attach evidence, and keep prose advisory. No workflow hook currently implements that contract.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Workflow-hook policy is high-judgment because it touches execution finalization and evidence. A weak implementation could block correct work on advisory prose findings or run too late for agents to repair obvious prose issues.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect docs Markdown changes in `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts.
- Run `ddx doc prose --changed` before finalization when docs changed.
- Attach prose-check evidence to the attempt.
- Feed findings back to the agent before final response when editable.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default and separate from correctness review.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make prose checks mandatory blockers.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.
- Do not alter Fizeau routing.

DEPS

","closing_commit_sha":"fa2b6cb58ec54164f2418f13f4b95c5298c1060f","created_at":"2026-05-18T03:36:42.627326328Z","description":"PROBLEM
store.go:Reopen (lines 1664-1665) and store.go:Unclaim (lines 1065-1067) maintain parallel loops that delete the same set of claim metadata Extra keys. This duplication is error-prone: if ClaimMetadataExtraKeys is extended, both loops must be updated.

ROOT CAUSE
store.go:Reopen clears claim fields in isolation; store.go:Unclaim does the same separately. Both iterate ClaimMetadataExtraKeys but the operations are not factored into a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Extract a private helper func clearClaimExtraKeys(extra map[string]any) that deletes all entries in ClaimMetadataExtraKeys from the Extra map.
2. Update store.go:Reopen:1664-1665 to call clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra).
3. Update store.go:Unclaim:1064-1069 to call clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra).
4. Verify both methods also call RemoveClaimHeartbeat as before (Unclaim does; Reopen should also).

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the set of keys being cleared. Do not refactor heartbeat cleanup.

+{"acceptance":"1. TestDefaultPluginPackageDoesNotAdvertiseProjectPayloadInstallTarget proves the built-in ddx package metadata no longer asserts .ddx/plugins/ddx as the forward install target, or names it only as legacy/local-overlay compatibility metadata.
2. TestBuiltinDDxPackageResolvesFromDefaultPluginFallback proves registry consumers can resolve built-in DDx resources through the embedded defaultplugin fallback without .ddx/plugins/ddx.
3. TestRegistryDistinguishesLocalOverlayFromMarketplacePackage proves explicit local overlays remain supported and are not confused with marketplace/cache-backed generated adapter behavior.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/frontend/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"0983465ec67228bfd5cdb29f16918e0ab957980e","created_at":"2026-06-16T19:56:55.136190208Z","description":"PROBLEM
The registry/defaultplugin layer still exposes .ddx/plugins/ddx as if it were the forward install target for the built-in DDx package. That makes compatibility metadata look like the normal marketplace/cache-backed topology and leaves tests asserting the wrong package shape.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:18-27 says generated adapters and payload copies are not forward repository state.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go:75-82 and cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/package.yaml:14-22 still expose install.root.target=.ddx/plugins/ddx for the built-in ddx package, while registry comments treat it as compatibility-only metadata.
- cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:60-68 asserts the legacy .ddx/plugins/ddx target.

PROPOSED FIX
Adjust registry/defaultplugin package metadata and tests so .ddx/plugins/ddx is either removed from forward assertions or explicitly classified as local-overlay/legacy compatibility metadata. Add tests that prove the built-in DDx package resolves through plugin lock/cache/defaultplugin fallback and that normal registry behavior does not require a project payload copy.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove support for explicit local development overlays. Do not remove the embedded default plugin fallback needed for offline ddx init. Do not change HELIX marketplace packaging.

PARENT

+{"acceptance":"1. Add `TestServerWarnsAboutOrphanHomeServerState` or the nearest package-appropriate named test proving startup emits a warning when `~/.ddx/server/` exists and the active `ddxroot.Path` destination is not `~/.ddx/`.
2. Add `TestServerWarnsAboutOrphanHomeTSNetState` or the nearest package-appropriate named test proving startup emits a warning when `~/.ddx/tsnet/` exists and the active state destination is not `~/.ddx/`.
3. The warning text identifies both the orphaned home path and the active state root so an operator can remove or migrate the stale directory intentionally.
4. `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"9b8393ac46ac6bf1ba2810b5e2102a56f6a76f37","created_at":"2026-06-07T08:42:14.66453622Z","description":"PROBLEM: After `ddxroot.Path($HOME)` stops returning `~/.ddx/`, existing incorrectly-created home state such as `~/.ddx/server/` and `~/.ddx/tsnet/` becomes orphaned. Operators need an explicit startup signal so stale TLS or tsnet state does not keep confusing client/server trust debugging.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` previously allowed `~/.ddx/` to act as a project state root. High-risk server consumers include TLS cert/key state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:326` and tsnet state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:503`. Once the resolver falls through to XDG, those old home directories are no longer read by the server path helpers.

PROPOSED FIX: Add startup detection in the server path that checks for legacy `JoinHome("server")` and `JoinHome("tsnet")` directories when the active project state root resolves elsewhere. Emit a clear warning naming the orphan source and the active XDG/project state destination. Keep the behavior warning-only unless the implementation already has an established safe migration helper.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rename `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/`. Do not address multi-project federation collisions. Do not change TLS-renew scheduling or rotation logic. Do not silently delete legacy home-state directories. Do not move unrelated user-global state such as `installed.yaml` or `plugins/`.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"readiness route No frontend component renders the ArtifactType definitions for an artifact. When an artifact matches a type definition (by prefix), the operator has no UI surface to view the associated template, prompt, and examples. The detail-view of any artifact is missing the type-aware panel.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/ contains artifact detail pages but no ArtifactTypePanel.svelte component.
- The typeDefinitions resolver (ddx-9ca4b5bf, a dep) provides the data, but nothing in the frontend consumes it.
- On prefix collision (multiple type definitions matching the same prefix), no selector dropdown or ?typeDef= URL state exists.
- prefixOf utility at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/artifacts/grouping.ts (from AC #10 of parent) is the expected import source.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/ArtifactTypePanel.svelte:
  - Tabbed layout: Reference Prompt | Template | Examples tabs (labels neutral, not 'Generate from').
  - On prefix collision: selector dropdown with ?typeDef= URL round-trip.
  - Imports prefixOf from artifacts/grouping.ts (single source of truth).
- Integrate ArtifactTypePanel into the artifact detail-view page.
- Add vitest unit tests for the component.

NON-SCOPE
- The typeDefinitions GraphQL resolver (that's ddx-9ca4b5bf, a dep).

7:-{"acceptance":"1. Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go with Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, string) signature.
2. PreClaimGuard and ComplexityGuard implement Guard; loop body passes them to nextCandidate.
3. attempted and hookFailed maps deleted from cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413; no references remain.
4. nextCandidate signature no longer accepts attempted map; filters via drain.result.Results instead.
5. Pre-claim hook denial calls w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown with 30s cooldown; hookFailed logic removed.
6. TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips in cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go: two consecutive Allow calls with hook error → second returns false.
7. TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce: nil ComplexityGate → one warning, allow proceeds.
8. Existing TestPreClaimHookDivergedSkipsBead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_preclaim_test.go still passes.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.181053449Z","description":"PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.","events_attachment":"ddx-06eb05d8/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-06eb05d8","issue_type":"task","labels":["kind:refactor","phase:2","reconciled:no-changes-state","refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result","updated_at":"2026-06-08T04:25:18.437797228Z"}

`ddx-0793ac75` is stale.   The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick.   - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.

reason: AC4's playwright clause cannot be satisfied inside this bead's scope; it requires the not-yet-implemented federation worker-control feature that ddx-28f3cf37 itself tracks separately.

Findings:

1. AC1-3 (narrow ddx-28f3cf37, name the four Go test gaps, cite reconciliation notes)
   are already satisfied. `ddx bead show ddx-28f3cf37` reads as a worker-control
   owner-targeted write bead (title "federation: owner-targeted worker-control writes"),
   names TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner,
   TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner,
   TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke, and
   TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID in its description, and its `notes` field
   cites FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177, TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338, and
   federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214. This reconciliation was already
   committed in 523e03b80 ("chore(beads): update ddx-28f3cf37 [ddx: doc-stamp]") prior
   to this execution. No further tracker edit is needed to satisfy AC1-3.

2. AC4's Go clause passes as-is:
   `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...`
   -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server/graphql (27.1s)
   -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation (2.2s)

3. AC4's playwright clause does not pass and cannot be made to pass within this
   bead's scope:
   `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts`
   -> 4 failed (starts a spoke worker from the hub, stops a spoke worker from the hub,
      refuses commands for an offline spoke, deduplicates retried forwarded worker commands)
   The spec file's own header (federation-worker-control.spec.ts:7-8) states: "These
   tests MUST FAIL until the hub federation workers view implements forwarded worker
   start/stop for spoke-owned projects." Making them pass requires implementing
   owner-targeted write forwarding across the GraphQL resolvers, the federation
   write-routing layer, and the Svelte workers-federation UI -- a feature-sized change
   that is exactly the work ddx-28f3cf37 exists to track, not something a bead titled
   "narrow the worker-control follow-up bead" is scoped to build. ddx-28f3cf37 remains
   open (unimplemented) as of this run, and this bead's NON-SCOPE section confines the
   work here to the bead-tracker text, not the write-routing implementation.

Recommendation: this bead's tracker-narrowing objective is already complete (AC1-3,
plus the Go half of AC4). The remaining playwright gap in AC4 will close naturally
once ddx-28f3cf37 is implemented and its four named Go tests plus the e2e spec go
green -- that is out of scope here. No code or bead-tracker change is being committed
by this run.

During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, a worker executing ddx-bdc0065e cancelled several stale beads. Multiple ddx bead update commands ran close together from the execute-bead context. The tracker ended clean, but the auto-commit audit trail was noisy: one commit subject said it updated ddx-387a0178 while it also picked up additional bead-row changes, another update failed with .git/index.lock, and another commit reported nothing to commit. That makes tracker history misleading and is the same class of dirty/index-lock noise the operator wants eliminated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:134-154 builds bead auto-commit config and calls bead.AutoCommitFilesWithRecovery directly. cli/internal/bead/git_autocommit.go:10-17 wraps git add/commit with gitlock.RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery, but that only recovers from stale/transient .git/index.lock; it does not serialize the whole add/commit pair under the process-shared main git lock. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:98-118 documents and implements withMainGitLock/withTrackerLock for primary-checkout git writes, and cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:22-24 says DDx writers serialize through that lock, but the bead CLI auto-commit path is outside it.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route bead CLI auto-commit add/commit operations through the same process-shared main git lock used by landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
- Preserve existing stale index.lock recovery inside the lock, so stale locks are still repaired and live owners still fail or wait according to policy.
- Add a concurrent regression where multiple bead update/create auto-commits run against one repo and produce either deterministic serialized commits or one explicitly combined commit; no index.lock failures, no misleading commit subjects that omit paths actually included.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change .ddx/beads.jsonl format.
- Do not remove auto-commit recovery for stale index locks.
- Do not squash, amend, or rewrite existing execute-bead history.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads

DDx internals still use `cli/internal/agent`, AgentRunRuntime, AgentRunner, ExecuteBead*, and related names for DDx-owned task execution. After the spec update, those names will be wrong: DDx owns task execution, while Fizeau owns routing/execution internals. Keeping `agent` as the DDx package name invites future routing-policy drift and confusion with real external AI agents.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/ contains DDx-owned queue drain, bead attempt, review, worktree, session evidence, and Fizeau adapter code. Existing refactor bead ddx-387a0178 still targets cli/internal/agent/try and cli/internal/agent/work and acceptance #7 keeps deprecated `ddx agent` aliases. Existing terminology bead ddx-3ee4ddcf still says not to remove legacy aliases. Those plans conflict with the no-deprecation decision and with FEAT-010 task-execution vocabulary.

PROPOSED FIX
After specs are ratified and the Fizeau contract cleanup release is integrated, split/rename DDx-owned internals to names matching responsibilities, for example `cli/internal/taskexec`, `cli/internal/taskexec/run`, `cli/internal/taskexec/try`, `cli/internal/taskexec/work`, `cli/internal/fizeauadapter`, and `cli/internal/runlog` or equivalent repo-consistent names. Rename exported/internal identifiers accordingly: AgentRunRuntime -> TaskRunRuntime or FizeauRunRuntime as appropriate; AgentRunner -> TaskExecutor/FizeauExecutor; ExecuteBead* -> Try/Attempt/Work terms. Keep `agent` only in literal external-agent concepts, historical event keys that cannot be migrated safely, or third-party standards names.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove the public `ddx agent` CLI in this bead unless it is already covered by ddx-d8474e0e. Do not change queue behavior, retry policy, review gates, or routing semantics. Do not implement Fizeau fuzzy matching. Do not rename durable tracker keys unless a migration plan is included in a separate bead.

rationale: status: proposed
reason: Bead scope far exceeds the single-attempt threshold (~500 lines / ~5 files). Actual scope: 100+ Go files in cli/internal/agent/ root package alone, plus subdirectories (try/, work/, executeloop/, escalation/, workerprobe/), plus 45+ cmd/ files, plus server/, GraphQL, and doc updates — all requiring coordinated rename. Decomposition depth cap (depth 2) prevents creating child beads. Operator must either (a) narrow this bead to a single slice (e.g., rename only cli/internal/agent → cli/internal/taskexec package declaration + import paths, one commit), or (b) retract this bead and file a sequenced set of depth-1 beads under a new parent. No code changes were made.

Scope evidence:
- cli/internal/agent/*.go: 100+ files (execute_bead_*.go alone: ~40 files; agent_runner*.go, candidate_cycle, dispatch, land, loop, review, triage, worktree, etc.)
- cli/internal/agent/try/, work/, executeloop/, escalation/: additional sub-packages
- cli/cmd/: 45+ agent-surface command files
- cli/internal/server/: GraphQL resolvers, HTTP handlers
- docs/: FEAT-010 (1375 lines), ADR refs, help text
- ACs 1–5 require cross-cutting audit + identifier rename across all of the above
- ACs 6–8 require all tests green after the rename

Decomposition depth cap: This bead is already at depth 2 (parent ddx-f088a3fd → this bead). A third decomposition layer is prohibited by the execute-bead instructions.

Recommended operator action:
1. Retract or close ddx-b194d20f as too-large-at-depth-2.
2. File new depth-1 children directly under ddx-f088a3fd, each scoped to one file group or one concern:
   - Slice A: Rename Go package declaration + module import path (cli/internal/agent → cli/internal/taskexec), fix all import sites.
   - Slice B: Rename exported identifiers: AgentRunRuntime → RunRuntime, AgentRunner → FizeauExecutor, ExecuteBead* → Try*, ExecuteLoop* → Work*.
   - Slice C: Update CLI command help text and server/GraphQL surface.
   - Slice D: Update docs, FEAT-010, and .agents/skills/ references.
   - Slice E: Close/supersede stale beads ddx-387a0178 and ddx-3ee4ddcf.
3. Each slice should be independently committable and testable in a single pass.

rationale: status: proposed
reason: Bead scope exceeds autonomous execution limit at decomposition depth 2 — cannot split further per the depth cap.

## What was assessed

Dependencies ddx-424fdac4 (spec ratification) and ddx-7065edd1 (Fizeau contract integration) are both **closed**; the bead is not blocked upstream.

## Why this cannot be executed in one pass

The bead requires satisfying AC1 ("DDx-owned production code no longer lives under `cli/internal/agent`") and AC5 (the grep returns only audited retained hits). Together these require a complete rename of the package:

- **200 Go files** inside `cli/internal/agent/` — each needs its `package agent` declaration updated to the new package name(s)
- **148 additional files** across the codebase that import `internal/agent` — each needs the import path updated
- **Hundreds of exported identifier renames** — `AgentRunRuntime`, `AgentRunner`, `ExecuteBeadResult`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, `ExecuteBeadReport`, `ExecuteBeadLoopStore`, `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadWithConfig`, and many more
- **Subpackage splits** — `escalation`, `executeloop`, `try`, `work`, `workerprobe` all need new paths under `taskexec`/`fizeauadapter`/`runlog`
- **All tests** must pass after the rename (`cd cli && go test ./...`)

Total affected files: ~350. Estimated lines changed: 10,000+. This is well beyond the ~500-line / ~5-file threshold.

## What a follow-up needs

Operator should choose one of:

1. **Scripted approach**: Run `gorename`, `sed`, and `gofmt` in a controlled sequence with checkpoint tests at each step. The rename can be scripted but needs iterative verification — not suitable for a single autonomous agent pass.
2. **Scope reduction**: File a new child bead narrowed to one concrete slice (e.g., only rename `cli/internal/agent/try/` → `cli/internal/taskexec/try/` and update that package's imports, leaving the main `agent` package for a subsequent bead).
3. **Manual + automation**: Operator runs `go tool` rename tooling locally, then dispatches a simpler verification bead.

## Stale beads (AC4) — no partial action taken

ddx-387a0178 and ddx-3ee4ddcf are both `open`. They should be closed as superseded by this bead's work, but since no implementation was completed it would be misleading to close them now. They remain open for the follow-up operator action.

All 21 ddx agent subcommands remain live in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85 — none are Hidden, none are Deprecated. The migration to ddx run/try/work was supposed to happen but only ddx work was added. Operators still use ddx agent surface, and skill manifests + docs still reference ddx agent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85: 21 cobra command registrations for ddx agent subcommands (run, execute-bead, work, benchmark, condense, capabilities, replay, list, doctor, log, etc.) are all live. None set Hidden=true or Deprecated.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: work and execute-bead are aliases; after C13 (ddx-387a0178), ddx work and ddx try replace these.
- .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, skills/: skill manifests reference 'ddx agent run' and 'ddx agent execute-bead'.
- docs/: README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website/content/docs/cli/_index.md reference ddx agent.
- Depends on C13 (ddx-387a0178) which adds ddx try CLI — cannot retire ddx agent until ddx try exists.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Delete cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and all cli/cmd/agent_*.go sibling files.
2. Verify: ddx run + ddx try + ddx work cover every prior ddx agent operation:
   - ddx agent run → ddx run
   - ddx agent work → ddx work
   - ddx agent execute-bead → ddx try
   - ddx agent list → ddx agent list removed (or migrated to ddx harness list)
   - ddx agent doctor → ddx doctor
   - ddx agent log → ddx log (or removed)
3. Update skill manifests to use new commands.
4. Update docs (README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website CLI docs).
5. Update lefthook hooks if any reference ddx agent.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding new flags or subcommands to ddx run/try/work (out of scope).

rationale: status: blocked
reason: AC #12 + AC #13 prerequisites unmet — ddx-ddacd4ff (C14) is open and the required function lifts are not in the codebase.

Verified blockers:

1. ddx-ddacd4ff (C14) status: open
   AC #13 of this bead states: "This bead's full retirement step inherits those lifts as prerequisites."
   ddx-ddacd4ff must be closed before ddx-d8474e0e can proceed.

2. agent.RecoverOrphans NOT lifted into try/work path.
   grep evidence: only call site is cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:168.
   Required destination (try.Attempt or ExecuteBeadWithConfig engine) has zero calls to RecoverOrphans.
   Deleting agent_execute_bead.go before the lift would silently drop orphan recovery.

3. agent.BuildLandingGateContext NOT in try path.
   Called at cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:206 but not in cli/cmd/try.go or try.Attempt.
   cli/internal/server/workers.go:1747 does call it, so server path is covered,
   but the cobra try surface does not invoke it.

4. agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService NOT in try path.
   Called at cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:161 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1515 (cobra wrappers)
   and cli/internal/server/workers.go:382 (server), but not in cli/cmd/try.go or work.go.

5. AC #10 required tests do not exist:
   - TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn  → missing in cli/cmd/try_test.go
   - TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding            → missing
   - TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead → missing
   - TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop → missing
   - TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2                 → missing
   These tests must be written (by ddx-ddacd4ff or a sibling bead) before the
   agent CLI surface can be retired.

What needs to happen before this bead can execute:
  a. Close ddx-ddacd4ff (C14): lift RecoverOrphans, BuildLandingGateContext,
     and ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService into ExecuteBeadWithConfig or try.Attempt.
  b. Add the five AC #10 tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go and cli/cmd/work_test.go.
  c. Re-queue ddx-d8474e0e after ddx-ddacd4ff is merged.

Manual verification (operator runs after merge): 'ddx try ddx-50da9674' (closed fixture-repo bead) returns non-zero with the closed-bead message; 'ddx try <some-ready-bead>' actually claims and executes it.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T20:06:45Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"ff5e64cecc17f3094f3933986966b94a50dc4f59","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:06:37.62309311Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent work' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.
- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep order.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:33:19.857924528Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T200645-f1713f26","harness":"claude","input_tokens":112,"output_tokens":52083,"total_tokens":52195,"cost_usd":8.31247625,"duration_ms":1590247,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:33:19.956008288Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=52195 cost_usd=8.3125"},{"actor":"erik","body":"preserve_ref=refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-f261d2a0/20260503T200645-f1713f26-88dde5dc9001

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

DEPS
None.   The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.

-{"closing_commit_sha":"2511a0b0de58e7ef136c5a7b032b2c0719103609","created_at":"2026-06-27T22:49:40.744689961Z","description":"PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

DEPS

  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.

NON-SCOPE

+{"closing_commit_sha":"2511a0b0de58e7ef136c5a7b032b2c0719103609","created_at":"2026-06-27T22:49:40.744689961Z","description":"PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.

The live read-federation coverage already proves the hub/spoke read path in `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation_test.go` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts`, so this bead now only needs to track the remaining owner-targeted write-routing gap.

ROOT CAUSE
`TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-350` already splits the work into TC-017 worker control and TC-018 bead writes. The read side is covered by `TestFederation_QueryShape_TwoHealthySpokes`, `TestFederation_PartialResult_OneSpokeOffline`, and `TestFederation_VersionSkew_RendersAndSkips`, which leaves only the owner-write routing pieces as durable follow-up work. The stale dependency edge to cancelled `ddx-387a0178` no longer belongs on the live queue.

PROPOSED FIX
- Narrow this bead so it names only the missing owner-write coverage.
- Preserve the FEAT-026 / Story 14 references.
- Keep the current read coverage cited explicitly so the queue does not treat the read path as pending work.
- Remove the blocking dependency on cancelled `ddx-387a0178` unless a note proves it is still load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement new federation code.
- Do not change the live read-federation behavior.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- Do not rewrite execution-audit commits.

-{"closing_commit_sha":"81958ef5baab9362ad0e85b1c481c0c5a20a4c21","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02.143711599Z","description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:migration, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f38727d22c22c139f37ea0bf08a99f437bd61cb3","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.569636417Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0b8780db","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-0b8780db","depends_on_id":"ddx-9f20a4bd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T05:05:24Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.

  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.
  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.
  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.
  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.
  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: heartbeat timing and lifecycle belong in one helper, not inline with candidate execution.
- P4: one bead heartbeat bug must not leak into the rest of the drain loop.
- P5: extracted heartbeat behavior supports always-visible worker state.","events":[{"actor":"","body":"","created_at":"2026-05-14T04:10:22.980177713Z","kind":"cooldown-cleared","source":"ddx work clear-cooldowns","summary":"operator-bulk-clear"}],"id":"ddx-0b8780db","issue_type":"task","labels":["kind:refactor","phase:2","refactor"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-05-09T13:09:06Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"open","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"proposed","needs-human-reason":"legacy lifecycle metadata required operator attention","needs-human-since":"2026-05-09T13:09:06Z","needs-human-source":"ddx bead migrate --lifecycle","needs-human-suggested-action":"review and accept, split, block, or cancel this proposed work","parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"manual-close-orphan-merge-2026-05-10","status":"closed","title":"C11: heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper","updated_at":"2026-05-14T04:10:22.980177713Z"}

reason: AC4's playwright clause cannot be satisfied inside this bead's scope; it requires the not-yet-implemented federation worker-control feature that ddx-28f3cf37 itself tracks separately.

Findings:

1. AC1-3 (narrow ddx-28f3cf37, name the four Go test gaps, cite reconciliation notes)
   are already satisfied. `ddx bead show ddx-28f3cf37` reads as a worker-control
   owner-targeted write bead (title "federation: owner-targeted worker-control writes"),
   names TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner,
   TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner,
   TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke, and
   TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID in its description, and its `notes` field
   cites FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177, TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338, and
   federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214. This reconciliation was already
   committed in 523e03b80 ("chore(beads): update ddx-28f3cf37 [ddx: doc-stamp]") prior
   to this execution. No further tracker edit is needed to satisfy AC1-3.

2. AC4's Go clause passes as-is:
   `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...`
   -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server/graphql (27.1s)
   -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation (2.2s)

3. AC4's playwright clause does not pass and cannot be made to pass within this
   bead's scope:
   `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts`
   -> 4 failed (starts a spoke worker from the hub, stops a spoke worker from the hub,
      refuses commands for an offline spoke, deduplicates retried forwarded worker commands)
   The spec file's own header (federation-worker-control.spec.ts:7-8) states: "These
   tests MUST FAIL until the hub federation workers view implements forwarded worker
   start/stop for spoke-owned projects." Making them pass requires implementing
   owner-targeted write forwarding across the GraphQL resolvers, the federation
   write-routing layer, and the Svelte workers-federation UI -- a feature-sized change
   that is exactly the work ddx-28f3cf37 exists to track, not something a bead titled
   "narrow the worker-control follow-up bead" is scoped to build. ddx-28f3cf37 remains
   open (unimplemented) as of this run, and this bead's NON-SCOPE section confines the
   work here to the bead-tracker text, not the write-routing implementation.

Recommendation: this bead's tracker-narrowing objective is already complete (AC1-3,
plus the Go half of AC4). The remaining playwright gap in AC4 will close naturally
once ddx-28f3cf37 is implemented and its four named Go tests plus the e2e spec go
green -- that is out of scope here. No code or bead-tracker change is being committed
by this run.

- `runInstall` (cli/cmd/install.go:54) reads the `--global` flag (install.go:49,57) and routes target resolution through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184). Ensure `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and global state, never the project tree. `ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` retried parent bead `ddx-c191e91a` after the parent had already been decomposed and marked execution-ineligible. On 2026-05-15 the first attempt `20260515T081727-1e2bf23c` preserved a no-change decomposition/parent update result, and `ddx bead show ddx-c191e91a --json` reported `execution-eligible: false` plus labels `area:agent,area:execution,kind:cleanup,decomposed`. `ddx bead ready --execution` no longer listed the bead. Despite that, the same live watcher started a second implementation attempt at `20260515T082640-623a9c00` against the same parent, burning model time and showing false progress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1073-1113` claims the candidate selected from the queue snapshot without a post-selection guard that rechecks execution eligibility after tracker changes made by a prior attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1681-1696` passes the originally selected `candidate` into `agenttry.Attempt`, so a stale candidate can still execute even if the store row has since been parked. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2367-2408` appends the terminal result and continues watch mode after a preserved/land-conflict/no-change result, allowing an immediate retry. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2603-2627` delegates selection to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, but the live incident proves this path can still select or retain an execution-ineligible decomposed parent. `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:131-134` documents the intended invariant: successful decomposition creates children and sets the parent `execution-eligible=false`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution-eligibility recheck immediately before claim and again before invoking `agenttry.Attempt` in the watch/drain loop. If a candidate is now execution-ineligible, decomposed, superseded, or otherwise absent from the fresh execution-ready view, release/skip it without dispatching an implementer and continue to the next ready bead or idle. Ensure preserved no-change decomposition and mixed no-change/land-conflict outcomes do not allow the same live watcher to retry the parent in the same loop run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go, only if needed for fixture reuse

NON-SCOPE
Do not change merge policy or preserved-iteration storage. Do not re-enable execution of decomposed parents. Do not hide land conflicts generally. Do not close the parent automatically unless existing child-closure policy already authorizes it.

DEPS
None. 4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"26bb0aec290f51bdab758ee99be54d9840a57366","created_at":"2026-05-27T05:50:45.963362833Z","description":"PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` can reject every pre-claim readiness result when `readiness_checks[].verdict` is emitted as a JSON bool instead of the consumer's expected string shape. In the observed Fizeau queue, each ready bead produced the same `pre_claim_intake.warn` decode error and the worker idled indefinitely despite ready work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The readiness classification decoder consumes `readiness_checks` through `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80`, then uses `failedReadinessReasons` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:823-834` to interpret failed checks. The contract must explicitly tolerate bool, string, null, and absent verdict values at this boundary; otherwise producer/consumer schema drift turns a readiness payload into `intake_error` before a bead can execute.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement or verify a custom `readinessVerdict` decoder that accepts `true`, `false`, strings, `null`, and absent verdicts, coercing to a documented canonical form (`true` -> `pass`, `false` -> `fail`, string -> trimmed string, null/absent -> empty). Ensure the classification decode path exercises that type and that failed-check detection still treats canonical `fail` as the failure signal.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau route or harness configuration. Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, CI schema locking, README documentation, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not rely on `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/...` as load-bearing test input; inline fixtures in tests. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
